49227.name: Base of the Stairwell
49227.description: You stand at the base of a stairway that spirals up into the mountain above you. The steps are cut straight out of the stone. The doorway to the east leads back into the cave that got you here in the first place.

49244.name: An Underground Cave
49244.description: You stand in a large underground cave. The only light comes from the hole far above in the ceiling that got you here in the first place. It is not nearly enough to light the whole area. As you stare at the hole and the surrounding area you realize there is no way you can get back to it, meaning you will have to find another way out.

49245.name: An Underground Cave
49245.description: You stand in the northwest of the cave. The walls curve around forming a rough circle. Small stones litter the floor, as well as a few larger ones. You have to navigate your way around the many stalagmites as you walk through the cave.

49246.name: An Underground Cave
49246.description: This section of the cave is riddled with stalagmites and stalactites, making it dangerous to navigate. The floor is slippery around the rock formations where water has dripped off. Small pools of water in shallow depressions smell stale with age.

49247.name: An Underground Cave
49247.description: The floor is littered with skeletons. While many of them come from animals, you see several that appear to have come from humans as well. You do not want to meet the creature that killed them. Especially not after you notice the tooth marks on the bones.

49057.name: In the Stairwell
49057.description: The stairway spirals its way up through the mountain. Because of the curve of the walls, you cannot see more than a few feet in either direction. Sconces adorn the wall every half turn of the stairwell, but they have long since gone dark.

49034.name: Intersection in the Tunnel
49034.description: The chamber is fairly small - more of an intersection of tunnels that a room in its own right. Hallways lead off in four different directions around you with torches lighting the way. An iron stairway rises from the center of the chamber into the tunnel above.

49035.name: Northern Tunnel
49035.description: The tunnel is fairly basic. Two walls, a ceiling, a floor. Torches light the way in both directions. You see a small burial chamber a short ways to the north, while the center of the catacombs lies to the south.

49036.name: Northern Burial Chamber
49036.description: The walls of the burial chamber are decorated with paintings and frescos of the man interred in the sarcophagus. Other burial goods lie around the room; items meant to ease the deceased's passage into death. The ceiling in the back of the room has collapsed blocking off the back wall and half burying the sarcophagus.

49037.name: Western Tunnel
49037.description: You walk carefully through the tunnel. Stalactites hang from the already low ceiling, making this a hazardous path to walk. You edge around the stalactites and get ready to be on your way. Small chambers lie in both directions.

49038.name: Western Burial Chamber
49038.description: The stalactites fade away as you enter a small, well-kept chamber. The only thing in the room is a sarcophagus against the back wall. The walls and ceiling are all bare, smooth rock.

49039.name: Eastern Tunnel
49039.description: Ancient stalactites hang from the ceiling over stalagmites just as old. They have been growing for long years as water drains through the ground into the tunnel. One stalagmite has actually grown up around a torch on the wall, leaving only the head visible.

49040.name: Eastern Burial Chamber
49040.description: This chamber was not well preserved. Water managed to leak through the ceiling and as a result stalactites and stalagmites have taken it over. You can barely make out old paintings on the wall in the gaps between them. The sarcophagus has been grown over completely. The center of the room is just one big column.

49041.name: Southern Tunnel
49041.description: The torches lining the walls flicker fitfully, giving off just enough light to see by. The tunnel widens out into the better lit intersection to the north. You cannot quite tell what lies to the south, the torches in that direction have died completely. You think you see movement, but it could just be your eyes playing tricks on you.

49042.name: The Queen's Burial Chamber
49042.description: The torches that once filled the room with light stand dark. They look to have been extinguished by a strong gust of wind, they hang on the wall at odd angles. A sarcophagus fills the center of the room, with various burial articles decorating the surrounding area.

49058.name: In the Stairwell
49058.description: The stairway spirals its way up through the mountain. Because of the curve of the walls, you cannot see more than a few feet in either direction. Sconces adorn the wall every half turn of the stairwell, but they have long since gone dark.

49136.name: A Vast Underwater Chasm
49136.description: The chasm through the sinkhole is quite vast, and very dark. For the most part light fails to reach these depths, though a few small rays break utter blackness. A slight glint in the deepest sands tickles the corner of your eye, but searching around quickly you see nothing out of the ordinary. The water seems strangely colder and altogether uncomfortable down here. The chasm continues westward - though exploring it seems a horrible idea - and the sinkhole leads closer to precious oxygen above you.

49137.name: Watery Grave
49137.description: Foolishly you forsake fresh air and land in favor of exploring a vast and dark underwater chasm. The water is even colder here than in the rest of the chasm, and your body quickly succumbs to hypothermia. The cold saps the strength from your muscles, and as you thrash wildly trying to escape, you only manage to tire yourself more. You open your mouth to scream in panic, and icy water fills your lungs.

49033.name: Beneath the Chapel
49033.description: You stand on a landing in a long spiral staircase. The stairs curve around in both directions so you can only see a few feet ahead of you. Torches hang from the wall every few feet, lighting the way so you can see where you are going. You can only wonder how they remain lit when all other evidence suggests you are the first one to walk these stairs in a long, long time.

49059.name: In the Stairwell
49059.description: The end of the stairway is in sight above. It leads out into open air, though you cannot see what lies around the exit until you get to the top. The stairs lead back down into the darkness below you.

49086.name: The Wine Cellar
49086.description: The cellar appears to have been built to store the wine made by the men who lived here. There are also several baskets along the wall that probably once held fruit, though anything in them has long since decayed to dust.

49108.name: An Old Basement
49108.description: Blood splatters all over the stone walls and floor of the basement. A semi- decayed corpse lies in one corner, still clutching a blood-soaked knife and bearing signs of horrible self mutilation. Boxes and barrels are stacked about the room, and as you consider investigating them, strange thoughts begin to flood your mind. You no longer seem curious about the prospect of cheap loot, but rather you begin to feel the desire to kill yourself...

49135.name: Under the Glassy Lake
49135.description: The water is perfectly clear under the lake. You can easily make out even the distant eastern shores. The water is quite chill, but poses you no immediate danger. However, submerging yourself in water in the middle of a wintery forest probably wasn't the best of ideas. Methodically, yet quickly, you search the bottom of the lake, and notice a sinkhole leading down into darkness at the deepest part of the lake bed.

49152.name: In the White Forest
49152.description: The snow glistening on the branches of the trees and in drifts on the ground makes your surroundings a truly beautiful sight to behold. One of the pine trees has grown to a very large size. It dwarfs most of the surrounding trees by a large margin.

49153.name: Inside the Tree
49153.description: There is just enough room beneath the branches for you to stand, and plenty of room for several people to stretch out on the ground. The surrounding branches reach all the way to the ground, completely blocking off the view outside the tree and providing shelter from the elements.

49154.name: In the White Forest
49154.description: You can actually see sky above your head as the forest thins and the trees are more spread out. The reason for this is apparent to the west, where the slopes of a mountain rise into the heavens to disappear among the clouds. The forest continues in all other directions.

49156.name: In the White Forest
49156.description: The forest is quite thin here, and off to the east you can hear a loud waterfall crashing swiftly onto rocks below. In fact, the trees have thinned out enough in places that you can make out the mighty white waterfall. To the north the mountains extend high into the sky, too steep and jagged to scale. To the west and south the forest stretches out, trees blocking your view.

49157.name: In the White Forest
49157.description: The snow isn't nearly as thick on the ground here as in other places, and in fact you can see patches of dead grass between stretches of frost and thin sheets of ice. The thin trees afford a view of a river off to the east, while the forest stretches on in all other directions.

49158.name: In the White Forest
49158.description: The frost and thin, brittle yet slick ice make the footing a bit treacherous here. You doubt falling would damage anything but your tailbone and your pride, but those are both things you'd like to keep intact. The snow gives way to dead and dying grass eastward as the land slopes downward to a slowly flowing river. Countless trees stretch out in all other directions.

49159.name: In the White Forest
49159.description: The forest is dead quiet except for the sound of rushing water off to the east. A thin layer of snow crunches under your feet, each footprint revealing frozen, dead grass beneath. Trees block your view beyond a few feet in every direction.

49160.name: In the White Forest
49160.description: Just north of the cobblestone path the trees are quite thin, and you can make out a river with sparkling blue water to the east, and an arching stone bridge spanning it just to the southeast. The trees are still a bit thin to the north, while they grow thicker to the west, and the cobblestone path lies a small distance to the south.

49161.name: In the White Forest
49161.description: The sheer mountains tower into the sky to the north, so steep and jagged that even the most experienced climber couldn't make it more than a couple of feet off the ground. The trees are a bit thin here due to the rocky ground near the mountain walls, but to the south the trees seem to be more abundant.

49162.name: In the White Forest
49162.description: A short way away from the monstrous mountains to the north, the forest thrives a bit more here. Many of the trees have died and fallen though, snow piled high on their trunks. The forest continues to the west and to the south, and is still a bit thin to the east.

49163.name: In the White Forest
49163.description: The forest is quite thick here, and it is hard to see far in any direction. Progress is slow, as you are forced to weave your way through the myriad trees, trudging through the snow and watching for any fallen branches and trunks, and trying to avoid any near-invisible ice patches.

49164.name: In the White Forest
49164.description: Snow crunching under your feet is the only sound that reaches your ears in the deep silence of the forest. Evergreens crowd in around you, so numerous that if you're not careful, you could easily lose your bearings. Every direction looks just about identical, and dead silence lies all about you.

49165.name: In the White Forest
49165.description: The thin cobblestone road is just visible to the south thanks to the trees that had been cleared to make the road's route. In every other direction, tall evergreens crowd in, preventing your gaze from penetrating very far into the forest.

49166.name: In the White Forest
49166.description: Standing on the eastern bank of the peaceful river, you take a moment to admire the beautiful waters. The trees are actually a few yards behind you as you face the river, and are quite thin back there. The river bends along to the north and east, while the forest stretches out to the south and west.

49167.name: In the White Forest
49167.description: To the north and east off in the distance you can hear the distinctive sound of a rushing river. As you peer through the trees to the south and west, it seems as though they thin out considerably, instead of growing in thicker as you would expect.

49168.name: In the White Forest
49168.description: The river courses swiftly to the north, though it wouldn't be too difficult to safely push a watercraft in. The forest is quite thin here and seems to remain that way to the east and west, yet to the south the trees seem to die out entirely.

49169.name: In the White Forest
49169.description: To the north and east the river winds, bending south as the waters rush downstream. A thin layer of snow and brittle ice cracks under your feet as you walk, though you can barely hear it this close to the river. Though they are swift, the white rapids do not look terribly dangerous.

49170.name: In the White Forest
49170.description: You stand at the brink of a gigantic impact crater. Minding your step near the edge, you gaze down to discern what appears to be a large meteor in the center. The crater is so large that you cannot further explore it. If you managed to survive the fall down, you likely wouldn't be able to find a way back out.

49171.name: In the White Forest
49171.description: An incredibly deep crater prevents any travel to the south, which is just as well, as all you can make out on the horizon is more jagged and snowy peaks. To the east you can make out the sound of a flowing river, and to the north a few thin trees still stand.

49172.name: In the White Forest
49172.description: Here a few dead trees have fallen into the river to the east. The river seems to be moving slowly enough to allow you to enter or cross without danger, as long as you are mindful of the strong currents caused by the thick trunks sticking out of the waters.

49173.name: In the White Forest
49173.description: It becomes a bit more difficult to navigate the forest away from the path. The snow is much deeper here, and following a straight line is impossible due to the sheer number of trees growing in close proximity to one another. It would be easy to lose your bearings here.

49174.name: In the White Forest
49174.description: The forest continues all around you in all directions save for the south, where the ground becomes much more rocky and the tall mountains begin to loom before you.

49175.name: In the White Forest
49175.description: The deep snow is forcing you to kick your legs high with each stride, only to sink several feet deep as you plant your boot for the next step. Your breath, coming in large puffs, crystallizes in the air in front of your face. The dropping temperature is becoming quite uncomfortable.

49176.name: In the White Forest
49176.description: As you trudge through the unmarked forest, you note that the foot of the mountains is close to the south in an attempt to keep your sense of direction. Their steep and jagged faces are beginning to seem a bit less oppressive than this blasted snow coated forest, but climbing them would be like raking your body across a bed of daggers. In all other directions the trees grow in thick.

49177.name: In the White Forest
49177.description: Trudging along, you almost don't notice the long drop that suddenly opened in the ground. One step further, and your foot would've met naught but air. It looks to be nothing more than a vast ditch, but the multitude of dead tree trunks could make the fall potentially lethal. To the north and west the forest appears navigable, assuming you remain cautious of course.

49178.name: In the White Forest
49178.description: To the west, the trees grow in too thick to make any progress, though the path lies in that direction. A little way off to the south, the mountains begin to tower into the sky, their peaks obscured by clouds. To the east and north the forest continues for unknown distances.

49179.name: In the White Forest
49179.description: As you make slow progress through the trees, you spot what appears to be a rabbit hole. Interesting that the delicate creatures could manage to make a home in such a hostile environment. Mindfully you check your weapon, realizing a confrontation with any predators that may reside in the area will likely result in a fight.

49180.name: In the White Forest
49180.description: Fallen trees and low branches do not make the journey through the forest any easier. Twigs reach out and claw at your face and clothes, while surface roots and long dead branches grab at your feet. The countless trees span out in every direction.

49181.name: In the White Forest
49181.description: You notice as you trudge along that the ground seems to slope downward to the southeast, though trees continue to grow in those directions and block your view. To the north, the forest seems to thin out a bit, and to the west the trees remain just as crowded as ever.

49182.name: In the White Forest
49182.description: To the south, any progress is halted by a large sink in the ground. A fall there would dash your body against countless tree trunks and would likely prove fatal. To the east, the strangest sight- a giant impact crater, like something had fallen from the heavens and crashed into the ground. The crater is so large that you cannot see the other side.

49183.name: In the White Forest
49183.description: The trees thin out a bit to the north and west, both directions in which the path winds. To the south however, the forest is thick and dark, and you have no desire to push in that direction. To the east the forest stretches on for as far as you can see.

49184.name: In the White Forest
49184.description: Minding your footing in the deep snow, you gaze south momentarily and notice that you can barely make out the vast and tall mountains now a bit distant in that direction. Too many trees now lie in between you and their rocky faces to get a good view.

49185.name: In the White Forest
49185.description: As you trudge along, you can just make out the sound of a thin sheet of ice cracking under your feet with each step. As if the footing wasn't treacherous enough with all this forsaken snow. The forest continues on in all directions, blocking your sight for more than a few meters.

49186.name: In the White Forest
49186.description: Some fallen branches and tree trunks slow your progress here. The trees seem quite a bit thinner here, and for a moment you can stop and enjoy the beauty of the forest. The ice-covered branches shimmer as light hits them, and the snow all around you glistens to dazzling effect. The forest seems a bit thinner to the east, but continues on as normal in all other directions.

49187.name: A Mysterious Clearing
49187.description: Without warning, you step into a large circular clearing. The area is completely devoid of plant life, and not nearly as much snow covers the ground. A few carved stone benches covered in a thin layer of snow are arranged in a circle towards the center of the clearing. It seems somewhat warmer here, but it could just be your mind playing tricks on you.

49188.name: In the White Forest
49188.description: The way to the east is blocked by an enormous impact crater, far too wide and deep to traverse. You doubt that you could climb your way back out if you fell in. The forest seems to continue on endlessly in all other directions.

49189.name: In the White Forest
49189.description: The path lies to the north and to the west, still visible from where you stand. To the south and east the forest seems to grow in much thicker, and you're unsure how much longer you can make progress in those directions.

49190.name: In the White Forest
49190.description: The trees seem quite a bit thinner than they are here to the north and the west. The ground under the snow seems frozen solid, and reminds you to watch your step. The forest to the east is far too thick to explore, as you doubt you have the strength to even make a few feet through the close growing trees.

49191.name: In the White Forest
49191.description: To the north and west the winding path offers easy access to other areas of the forest. The trees due south also appear somewhat thin, yet to the east and southeast, the forest has grown rampant, and you can see nothing but a wall of branches and trunks.

49192.name: In the White Forest
49192.description: The trees are quite thick here, almost too thick to navigate through. Each step is heavily labored as branches and roots grab at you from all directions. To the north and west, the forest does begin to grow in too thick to fit more than a limb through the branches.

49193.name: In the White Forest
49193.description: To the south and east a gigantic impact crater prevents you from exploring further. However, the forest grows in thick to the north and west, and though you can't see much, they're better options than hurling yourself to your likely demise.

49194.name: In the White Forest
49194.description: To the north and west the thick patch of trees stops you from even considering trying to make progress in those directions. To the south and east the forest is still densely populated by trees, but not so much as to completely prevent travel.

49195.name: In the White Forest
49195.description: Your only options for travel here are the thick forest to the north and west, as to the south and east lies the mysterious giant crater. Idly you wonder how many dead, crushed trees are buried under the snow that settled since the massive crater was formed. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering.

49196.name: In the White Forest
49196.description: The sound of snow crunching under your feet has begun to sound like some sort of mantra, rhythmic and soft. To the east, a blotch of blackness obstructs your view as the branches of countless trees wind together. The forest appears relatively inviting in all other directions.

49197.name: In the White Forest
49197.description: To the north you can make out the junction of two forest paths, as the path to your west meets up with another that leads off in some other direction. To the east the forest stretches on, and to the south the trees grow in far too thick to allow exploration.

49198.name: In the White Forest
49198.description: Snow drifts lightly from overhead, the snowflakes thicker than you have ever seen. You must shake your cloak off every few steps lest your clothes become coated in a layer of fresh snow. To the north you can make out the path that winds through the forest, and to the east and west the forest continues. The south is blocked by a particularly thick growth of trees.

49199.name: In the White Forest
49199.description: Under your feet the snow glistens like thousands of tiny flashing lights. It would be even more beautiful if it wasn't so blasted cold. To the north you can make out the cobblestone path which would allow for easy travel across the forest. To the south the trees grow in too thick for you to make any progress, but the same obstacle does not exist to the east or west.

49200.name: In the White Forest
49200.description: The trees are a bit thin here, a small distance away from the forest path. Looking about, you're afforded a view of naught but trees in all directions. They all look pretty much the same. Try not to get lost.

49201.name: In the White Forest
49201.description: To the north and west you can make out the path just beyond a line of trees, and off in the distance to the northeast you can just hear the distinctive sound of rushing water. The trees continue to the south and east preventing you from observing your surroundings for more than a few feet.

49202.name: In the White Forest
49202.description: Snow piles high and slopes gently away from a ten foot tall stone wall to the north. Looking along the wall, you can make out a large iron gate to the west, where the path that snakes through the forest lies. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light.

49203.name: Northwest Corner of the Chapel
49203.description: The trees have grown almost right up against the side of the chapel. There is barely enough room to squeeze between the walls and the trees to get by. The wall is covered in moss and riddled with cracks. The forest is impenetrable to the west and north but you can follow the walls of the building around to the east and south.

49204.name: North of the Chapel
49204.description: You lose your footing as you try to walk down the steep slope from the west. There is a deep pit in the ground along the back wall of the chapel. You cannot tell how recently it happened, but the stains on the wall of the chapel below the usual ground level show that the pit was not always there.

49205.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49205.description: The mountain you stand before now is far less rocky than the one you see to the north, it even looks as if you could climb this one a ways as long as you took care in doing so. You cannot help but marvel as you look around at the sheer size of the mountains stretching into the distance.

49206.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49206.description: The mountainside slopes gently upward for a ways before becoming gradually steeper the farther you progress up it. The mountain chain continues as far as you can see to the north and south. As you look east you see nothing but trees which block your view of whatever else might be there.

1079.name: An Underground Cave
1079.description: You stand in a large underground cave. The only light comes from the hole far above in the ceiling that got you here in the first place. It is not nearly enough to light the whole area. As you stare at the hole and the surrounding area you realize there is no way you can get back to it, meaning you will have to find another way out. who group Your group consists of:

1080.name: An Underground Cave
1080.description: You stand in a large underground cave. The only light comes from the hole far above in the ceiling that got you here in the first place. It is not nearly enough to light the whole area. As you stare at the hole and the surrounding area you realize there is no way you can get back to it, meaning you will have to find another way out. n;break pile An Underground Cave You stand in the northwest of the cave. The walls curve around forming a rough circle. Small stones litter the floor, as well as a few larger ones. You have to navigate your way around the many stalagmites as you walk through the cave.

49209.name: The Base of the Cliff
49209.description: Travel to the west is blocked by the cliff rising straight up above you where the rock fell away from the mountain. The rubble from the landslide stretches away to the east where the forest was swept away in the disaster. The cliff continues away to the south for some distance.

49210.name: The Base of the Cliff
49210.description: The cliff face blocking your way to the west continues away to the north, the result of a major landslide many years ago. The rock from the disaster fell away to the east, destroying a large section of forest. To the south of your location the mountain range curves around to the southeast.

49211.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49211.description: The mountain rises away above you, so high that the peak is hidden among the clouds. You can only imagine what the view from the top would be like on a clear day, as to try to climb it would be suicide. The mountainside is treacherously steep and covered in snow and ice.

49212.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49212.description: You begin to grow tired of craning your neck looking up at the slopes of mountains. They stretch away both ahead of you and behind. Unfortunately the only other option is to crane your neck staring at the enormous trees that are blocking you from going in any other direction.

49213.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49213.description: You stand at the base of a huge mountain that stretches away into the sky above you. The forest to the north is far too thick for you to be able to push your way through, but back to the east you can see the remains of a path. It is thickly overgrown, but still clear enough to traverse.

49208.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49208.description: There are boulders scattered across the side of the mountain, interspersed with a few trees that have grown out past the edge of the forest. The mountains to the south end in sheer cliff faces where huge landslides tore away the edges of the mountains.

49215.name: In the White Forest
49215.description: The snow is deep as you trudge through this part of the forest. You have to force your way through it to continue on your way. Mountains block your way to the north, but the forest continues in all other directions.

49216.name: In the White Forest
49216.description: The brush thickens here. You have to fight for every step you take, with branches and bushes tearing at you all the way. As you look to the east you know you will not be able to push your way any father. The forest gets too thick. The way north is blocked by the mountains.

49217.name: In the White Forest
49217.description: The forest here is beautiful. This close to the mountains it is still fairly thin, making it easy to navigate. The light glitters off of the snow and ice adorning the trees branches. The few branches that have broken off and fallen to the ground serve only to give the ground some shape, creating large snow drifts.

49218.name: In the White Forest
49218.description: The trees are spread out in an almost uniform pattern. While it gets denser the farther south you go, it still holds to the same basic pattern. To the east, you see the tops of the mountains rising above the trees.

49219.name: In the White Forest
49219.description: You are forced to slow your pace more and more as the forest thickens. The forest to the south is completely impenetrable. The trees are somewhat less dense to the other directions.

49220.name: In the White Forest
49220.description: The trees towering above you stretch away in all directions. If not for the fact that you knew the densest forest was to the south, you would have no idea where you were. You shiver from the cold, seeing no respite from it anywhere nearby.

49221.name: In the White Forest
49221.description: The trees are so dense here that you cannot see more than a few feet in any direction. You spin around in a circle as you realize you do not know which direction you came from. Even your footprints have already disappeared. You turn in another circle as you hopelessly try to determine where you were going.

49222.name: In the White Forest
49222.description: You stumble as you trip over something buried in the snow. You do not know whether it was a rock or branch or something else, but it does not matter. What you do know is that you are cold and wet and would like nothing more than to settle down in front of a nice warm fire somewhere indoors.

49223.name: Atop the Rubble
49223.description: You walk on top of the rubble from the great landslide off of the mountain to the west. You stand at a higher elevation than the rest of the valley as a result, so you are able to see across the tops of the trees to the far side of the valley. It is a beautiful site, snow covered trees with a few buildings spotting the landscape.

49224.name: Atop the Rubble
49224.description: You stand at the end of the long pile of rubble resulting from a landslide long ago. The rubble swept the forest clear for a long distance, but the trees are now starting to reclaim the lost ground. The edges of the pile are overgrown with moss and small trees have started to grow up among the rocks.

49225.name: Atop the Rubble
49225.description: The rubble from the rockslide swept away the trees for a fair distance into the forest. You have to pick your path carefully as you walk through the rubble, as many of the stones are loose and shift beneath your feet. The forest picks up again some distance to the east.

49226.name: Atop the Rubble
49226.description: The trees here at the southeastern corner of the rockslide have grown right up over the edge of the rubble in the attempt to reclaim the area. The forest is thick along the edge, blocking you from progressing any further into it.

49228.name: In the White Forest
49228.description: The winding forest path curves around the area to the west and south. Off to the north, you can make out a tall stone wall through the thin evergreens. The forest appears to continue to the east, with all too familiar scenery.

49229.name: In the White Forest
49229.description: You're hardly in the forest at all, really. To the west is a tall stone wall that has gathered a steep snow bank. The wall itself extends all the way to the face of the mountains to the north. To the north and east, the rocky mountains tower into the clouds. The only exit lies to the south, back into the forest.

49230.name: In the White Forest
49230.description: There are few trees here, this close to the mountains. Nearby to the northwest is a wall made of stone. Off to the east you can still make out the path that runs through the forest. The mountains crowd in to the northeast, while a glimpse to the southeast affords a view of evergreens.

49231.name: In the White Forest
49231.description: The forest is quite thin here, and a quick glance at your surroundings explains why. A short distance to the north the forbidding mountains are in plain sight. The ground under the snow at your feet is likely more rock and ice than soil.

49232.name: In the White Forest
49232.description: To the south and west, the path that gives at least some direction in this accursed forest is easily in reach. Alternatively, you can wind through trees and trudge through deep snow and slip on ice in every other direction. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering.

49233.name: In the White Forest
49233.description: The mountains loom to the north, and there are few trees in the area. All around you lies the silence of the forest. The sparse evergreens stand in stark contrast to the biting cold, the rocky mountain face, the endless blanket of snow and the clear, slick ice. This truly is a land of death.

49234.name: In the White Forest
49234.description: A few fallen trees here and there impede your progress, forcing you to choose between walking around them or climbing over them. Both are unpleasant tasks in this snow and cold. The forest continues in all directions.

49235.name: In the White Forest
49235.description: The oddly well maintained path lies to the south, and offers an easy way out of the trees and deep snow. It even offers a way out of the whole forest if you follow it far enough to the west.

49236.name: In the White Forest
49236.description: To the north the mountains rise high into the heavens, their peaks obscured from view by clouds and grey skies. The mountains stretch on to the west, and sparse trees lie to the south. To the southeast you can make out some sort of large structure.

49237.name: In the White Forest
49237.description: You stand near a large stone building two floors tall. Small windows, all broken in more places than are in-tact, stand in the wall at regular intervals. The building stretches on quite a way to the south.

49238.name: In the White Forest
49238.description: Just to the south you can make out the winding forest path, which seems to end at the entrance of the stout stone building to the east. In every other direction evergreens grow sparsely.

49239.name: In the White Forest
49239.description: The sheer, rocky mountains prevent any progress to the north, and the tall stone building stretches nearly the entire way to the mountains. However you are able to walk quite comfortably here, as there are no trees to slow your progress for once.

49240.name: In the White Forest
49240.description: You stand at an exterior corner of the stone building. To the south and east the building looms above your head, and to the north the mountains crowd in. There are no trees growing in this cramped space, and the snow is not deep. The building comes to a sharp corner almost touching the mountains to the northeast, but it looks like you might be able to squeeze through.

49241.name: In the White Forest
49241.description: It is quite cramped here, not quite big enough for you to walk without scraping your shoulders on either the stone building or the face of the mountain every other step. To the southwest you could squeeze past the building and head back into the forest, and to the east the cramped passage continues.

49242.name: In the White Forest
49242.description: A tight fit, but at least the snow is light and there are no trees. The ground under your feet feels like rock coated in ice, but you're not sure that if you lost your footing you'd fall without just getting stuck in between the building and the mountain.

49243.name: In the White Forest
49243.description: The mountains now begin to bend southward, and block the way to the north and east. It looks like a rockslide occurred recently, as much of the rubble is stacked against the stone structure. The building itself still pushes up close to the mountains, and it looks like your only exit is back through the cramped passage to the west.

49300.name: Forest at the Cliff
49300.description: In the shadow of the huge black cliffs to the north, the sparse, gaunt trees are bowed low under the weight of the season's heavy snowfall. As you navigate your way between them, disturbing the careful balance, snow is catapulted into the air as they spring upright, sprinkling back down upon you.

49301.name: Forest at the Cliff
49301.description: Snow weighs heavily on the boughs of weary trees, drooping deeply in the frigid air. The shadow of the black cliffs above you only adds to the somber atmosphere, broken now and again as a drift of snow drops from its perch on a ledge to land with a thud only a few feet from where you were standing last.

49302.name: Forest at the Cliff
49302.description: Glistening drifts of white snow build and erode against the unassailable cliffs to the north, constantly shifting in size and position. Some of the tallest firs are half buried by the largest of them, while you imagine there are many smaller ones smothering beneath the freezing white powder.

49303.name: Forest at the Cliff
49303.description: A huge boulder has, quite recently, dislodged itself from the cliff face and come crashing down amongst the trees, shattering them like so much kindling. The jagged trunks jut out from the snowy crater at haphazard angles, still dripping with sap.

49304.name: Forest at the Cliff
49304.description: The air between the trees shimmers like a kaleidoscope, filled with snow tossed up by eddies whipping between the tall pines, kicked up by your passing. The black cliffs loom to the north, but appear to turn sharply just ahead.

49305.name: In the White Forest
49305.description: The trees at the edge of the forest, looming darkly to the east, are sparse and young. The area appears to have been cleared some time ago, but is slowly being reclaimed by the ever encroaching wood. Off to the northwest somewhere, the shape of a building, or some manmade structure, can be seen through the thin, snow-covered boughs.

49306.name: In the White Forest
49306.description: You stand in the shadow of an ancient pine - or what is left of it. Struck by lightning who knows how long ago, the shattered trunk still towers several dozen feet above your head, and would require at least three or four large men to link their arms around it. Here and there misplaced mounds of snow indicate where other pieces may have landed.

49307.name: In the White Forest
49307.description: Dark evergreens, heavy with snow, cluster tightly about you and make travel uncomfortable. Any misstep results in a sloppy fall, and a face - or worse yet boot - full of the cold wet stuff. As if that weren't enough, each and every branch seems to be just waiting to drop its payload directly on top of your head, and down the back of your neck.

49308.name: In the White Forest
49308.description: The crunch of your footsteps, though muffled by the snow, is the only sound that disturbs the chilly silence amongst the trees. The tall firs and pines, interposed here and there by a mighty cedar or shapely spruce crowd tightly together in the frigid air.

49309.name: In the White Forest
49309.description: Tiny flakes dance between the boughs of slender evergreens, crowded close together and weighed heavy with snow. Drifts shift and blow, building here, dwindling there, against the trunks of the dark trees. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering.

49310.name: In the White Forest
49310.description: Just to the west a stone bridge spans a thin river, joining the forest path which continues to each side of the river. However the bridge is several feet about the river, and out of reach. Heading west would take you into the river itself. The forest path winds away to the north, and forest stretches in all other directions.

49311.name: In the White Forest
49311.description: The river bends near here, and you can see it plainly to the west and south. The trees are few here, and the water calm enough to easily push off into the river, though it seems to pick up speed as it moves downstream to the south.

49312.name: In the White Forest
49312.description: The forest path curves along to the north and west, offering a bit of direction. A small salvation, though perhaps a better option than getting lost among the endless trees of the forest and freezing to death. Those trees happen to continue with no discernable end to the east and south.

49313.name: In the White Forest
49313.description: Surrounded by evergreens, you find yourself unable to do much but listen to the silence of the forest, hug your arms a bit tighter to yourself, and pick a direction that looks the same as every other.

49314.name: In the White Forest
49314.description: The river still courses calmly to the south. You can hear the gentle trickle of water as it passes by, a welcome relief from the usual noise of boots crunching on snow. It'd be easy to row out into the river from here, or wander about in the forest which continues in every other direction.

49315.name: In the White Forest
49315.description: You stand right near the path of the forest as it curves all about you, to the north, east, and west. The evergreens grow in a bit thicker to the south.

49316.name: In the White Forest
49316.description: Though the snow covered trees block your view for much but a few feet, you're sure the forest path is around here somewhere. There are no landmarks here. No structures, not the sound of water, even the mountains are out of view as numerous trees tower around you.

49317.name: In the White Forest
49317.description: Every bit of forest is starting to look exactly the same. Not even any fallen or dying trees to distinguish your position from any other. Looking back, you notice the snow already beginning to conceal your footprints.

49318.name: In the White Forest
49318.description: A little off to the southwest you can make out the quiet trickle of a gentle river. Aside from that, there is nothing else to provide you with a sense of direction. Only endless evergreens.

49319.name: In the White Forest
49319.description: The forest grows right on to the bank of the river to the south, as if the trees are trying to grow in the river itself. Ice cracks under your feet as you approach the bank, and the water must be bone cold, but you could still easily shove a boat into the river and enjoy a different bit of scenery.

49320.name: In the White Forest
49320.description: The forest path is visible to the north and east as it winds through the length of the forest. In all other direction, expanses of snow and ice covered trees look unwelcoming.

49321.name: In the White Forest
49321.description: The forest surrounds you in all directions, though you can make out a gap in the trees a bit to the northeast, indicating the path must be nearby in that direction.

49322.name: In the White Forest
49322.description: To the east, a large and circular stone structure stands in the middle of the forest. Though from this distance you can make out no doors anywhere on the structure. In all other directions, the forest stretches on with little variation from what you have yet seen.

49323.name: In the White Forest
49323.description: Just to the southeast the sound of a river is quite clear. In fact, it's a little odd you didn't hear it from even further away with how quiet the forest is. Snow crunches and ice cracks under your boots as you turn about to see every direction looking the same, but the sound of the river at least provides a kind of landmark.

49324.name: In the White Forest
49324.description: Here the forest grows up close against the river, with some even dipping into the water with their branches. Perfect, undisturbed crystals of ice have formed on most of these branches, with light snow dusting the top, looking very much like the heavens have been showering precious jewels on the simple evergreens.

49325.name: In the White Forest
49325.description: A few trees grow close to the river, but not much elsewhere as the mountains rise to the south. Not anywhere near their true height, they still raise several feet into the sky, steep, jagged, and covered in ice. Passage further south is impossible without heading into the river which over the years has carved its own way.

49326.name: In the White Forest
49326.description: Just north of here snakes the well kept path that guides your way through the expansive forest. Yet of more interest is a circular stone building a short distance to the south. It is certainly not something one would expect to find in this isolated forest, especially with no grandeur surrounding it.

49327.name: In the White Forest
49327.description: Nearby to the north is strange, circular stone structure, nearly hidden in the forest. From here you can see that drifted snow has piled up against it, and like everything else in these woods it isn't likely anyone has been there in a while. In all other directions, snow covered evergreens block your view for more than a few feet.

49328.name: In the White Forest
49328.description: This far from the path you can make out the mountains beginning to rise in the distance to the south. It doesn't appear as though they reach their potential height as soon as the peaks to the north, but surely they will reach into the forest soon.

49329.name: In the White Forest
49329.description: The mountains begin here to the south and east. While not as intimidating as the sheer faces of the northern peaks, the mountains here are still jagged and covered in ice. Any attempt at climbing them would surely dash you on the rocks below, and a fall from just a few feet up could be lethal here. Your only options lie in exploring the forest stretching in every other direction.

49330.name: In the White Forest
49330.description: The cobblestone path lies just north of here. As if it wasn't strange enough that it is somehow well maintained, it looks as though it is slightly illuminated by the circular glow of man-made light. In all other directions you see nothing but frost covered trees.

49331.name: In the White Forest
49331.description: To the west a strange structure apparently perfectly circular stands as if hidden among the evergreens of the forest. It must've taken a great deal of work to construct all the way out here without disturbing the surrounding forest. In the White Forest

49332.name: In the White Forest
49332.description: The expanse of evergreens seems to thin out about here as large rocks peek through the snow on the ground. It would almost be a welcome change of scenery if it didn't mean the peaks that are nearby to the south certainly do not offer any way out of here.

49333.name: In the White Forest
49333.description: Jagged rocks strangled by shimmering ice begin their ascent to the heavens, cutting off any progress southward, away from the forest. You will find no exit here, or anywhere near by the looks of the mountains that stretch without end out of sight to the south, and the numerous trees in all other directions.

49334.name: In the White Forest
49334.description: Just north of here you can easily discern the entrance to some large, square- shaped stone building. At the very least it may offer some shelter from the elements and recall some sense of civilization into your mind, be it a pathetic and broken shell of the glory of other Isharian lands.

49335.name: In the White Forest
49335.description: Snow continues to drift lightly from the grey sky, some landing gently upon your face and shoulders, but most falling to the ground or bending trees as it accumulates atop their branches. The sight is dazzling, almost enough to take your mind off this biting cold of this hellacious maze.

49336.name: In the White Forest
49336.description: To the east, a large stone alcove seems to just a good distance away from a larger two story building. The entrance to the building must be around here somewhere, but all you can see is forest.

49337.name: In the White Forest
49337.description: The mountains loom to the south, every bit as silent as the forest, but much more lifeless. Towering into the grey skies above, they offer no way out of the frozen forest.

49338.name: In the White Forest
49338.description: You stand in the corner of a tall stone building. Not many trees here, as it looks like they have been cleared away for building materials or firewood. Most of the windows are broken, but you should still probably look for the front door.

49339.name: In the White Forest
49339.description: A large stone alcove juts out a great distance from the tall stone building, putting you in an exterior corner of the structure. Looking in through the broken windows of the alcove, it appears to be some sort of library.

49340.name: In the White Forest
49340.description: Only a few feet separate the large alcove to the north from the mountains to the south, and a sunny day with blue skies would likely provide a beautiful view from inside the building. However, that isn't the case, and the jagged mountains look as uninviting as the decaying stone structure. It looks as though the forest continues east and west.

49341.name: In the White Forest
49341.description: You stand near the corner of some large stone building. Peering in through the broken windows you can see a long hallway to the north and some sort of large library to the west. Evergreens block your view of anything else in every other direction.

49342.name: In the White Forest
49342.description: Forest much like you have seen thus far stretches on as far as you can see. Through the trees you can make out the rocky mountains nearby to the southwest, and a little more distant directly south.

49343.name: In the White Forest
49343.description: The west and south are blocked by the slick and jagged formations of the mountains that begin to rise a bit more slowly than their neighbors farther north. They seem to continue some distance to the east.

49344.name: In the White Forest
49344.description: You stand near the wall of the tall stone building, broken and abandoned. Peering in through the windows you notice there is no snow on the floor inside the building, which might be a welcome change for your poor freezing feet. Though you probably shouldn't cut yourself up trying to scramble over broken glass.

49346.name: In the White Forest
49346.description: The mountains loom ominously to the south, and the evergreens are few and stunted. Ice cracks deafeningly under your boots, reminding you of the horrible silence that has haunted you since you entered this cursed place.

49347.name: In the White Forest
49347.description: To the north is a tall stone building, but clearly this is just a hallway and the front door must be somewhere else. Still, inside may offer a bit of shelter from the cold and the snow, which might just lift your spirits. At least there's no forsaken trees in there.

49348.name: In the White Forest
49348.description: At least you can get a clear view of your surroundings in spite of all the evergreens here. To the north you can make out a tall building made of stone, and to the south and east mountains tower into the sky. Gazing west, you see yet more trees. A daemonic assassin's eyes roll back in his head as a result of your wicked backstab! (70) A daemonic assassin issues a bloodcurdling cry of pain! You store a sulphuric crystal in your component pouch.

49349.name: In the White Forest
49349.description: To the east and south the cold and jagged rocks of the mountains stop your progress, and you're briefly reminded of a caged animal. To the north and west you can see nothing beyond a few feet of trees. r You lie down and relax.

49350.name: In the White Forest
49350.description: To the north the stone building seems built all the way in almost to the mountainside itself, and you are unable to make any progress in that direction or to the east. To the west you can see that the long outer wall of the building continues for some distance.

49351.name: In the White Forest
49351.description: Ice cracks under your feet and covers the faces of the mountains to the east and south as they rise to the grey skies above. Thanks to the thinning of the forest you can make out a stone building to the north, but nothing but evergreens lie to the west.

49360.name: In the White Forest
49360.description: There are few trees here, this close to the cold and jagged face of the northern mountain range. To the west you can hear water crashing down from somewhere high in the rocks above. To the east and south the forest extends still with a thin cover of evergreens.

49361.name: In the White Forest
49361.description: The mountains block any progress to the north, which is just as well. It must be barren wasteland any farther north than here. In all other directions a few evergreens attempt a meager living, as far as plants go.

49362.name: In the White Forest
49362.description: A tall stone wall is built all the way into the mountains here. Snow banks accumulate along the wall, drifted here by wind. The wall extends a short distance to the south, and likely has some sort of opening nearby. Yet more trees grow to the west and south.

49363.name: In the White Forest
49363.description: To the west a river rages violently. The water would likely be perfectly clear, having filtered through all the rocks to the north, but the strength of the waterfall creates churning white rapids. You could probably push your boat into the river from here, but it isn't the safest of places to attempt such a thing.

49364.name: In the White Forest
49364.description: Surrounded by forest, you are grateful to be so close to the mountains. To the north you can see them towering into the grey skies, serving as a landmark to help you keep your bearings. Aside from that, everything looks exactly the same in every direction.

49365.name: In the White Forest
49365.description: The well kept forest path is visible just to the east, and it appears to stop before a tall gate built into a stone wall. At least there is less snow to trudge through along the path.

49366.name: In the White Forest
49366.description: You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here. It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north. It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest.

49367.name: In the White Forest
49367.description: Forest stretches in all directions, though you can faintly hear the sound of water off to the southeast. The ground is a little uneven here, and every now and then the snow hides holes and small ledges in the ground. A small inconvenience, but not at all pleasant when your whole leg sinks into the snow.

49368.name: In the White Forest
49368.description: The forest path curves around you to the east and south, a welcome sight. It is certainly better than trudging through the deep snow and trying to navigate through the countless trees which make it impossible to keep a straight line. To the north and west, snow-heavy evergreens block your view for more than a few feet.

49369.name: In the White Forest
49369.description: To the west, the river courses gently downstream. It would be quite a peaceful boat ride and a brief escape from snow and trees. The forest path to the east and south offers similar relief and the chance at a gentle stroll, as opposed to a straining trudge.

49207.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49207.description: The trees thin out as the edge of the forest meets up with the base of the mountain to the west. To the east, though, they thicken again to the point of being impassable. The mountains continue to rise into the sky in the other directions.

49214.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49214.description: The mountains here start to curve around to the north and east, blocking you from progressing west or north. The forest to the east is fairly thin here, leaving you plenty of room to wander through it if you wanted to travel in that direction.

49000.name: A Shattered Gate
49000.description: A twisted and broken black gate hangs on one hinge. Although it is a mere shell of what it used to be, it was clearly never meant to be very ornate. It appears to have been in this condition for some time, though oddly there is not a single spot of rust. The slightly overgrown path leading up to the gate continues beyond it, though you can see nothing else through the dense forest of evergreens.

49001.name: Overgrown Path
49001.description: The path seems to have been in disuse for several years, save that there lies a bit less snow on the path than off it. It is unnaturally cold past the gate, and a deathly chill creeps in through your cloak. To every side of the path trees grow thick, limiting your view into the forest to no more than five feet at any given location. The path leads on northward, though to the northeast the trees seem to be a tad bit less dense. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering.

49002.name: Overgrown Path
49002.description: Snow drifts ever so lightly from the skies, and the forest seems so breathtakingly beautiful. The snow at your feet sparkles as if laced with millions of tiny diamonds. No footprints disturb any of the snow around you, save your own. However, the path seems to hold its shape, suggesting someone must care for it lest it be utterly reclaimed by nature.

49003.name: Overgrown Path
49003.description: Barely into the forest, the trees crowd the path quite tightly here, and seem to block off all sound. The only things you can hear are your own footsteps, your own breathing. The tranquility seems unnatural, and not at all peaceful. It is almost as if a forced silence pervades the area.

49004.name: Overgrown Path
49004.description: Small roots stretch across the path only a few inches above the ground, minding you to watch your step lest you end up looking foolish. The path begins to curve gently to the northeast, and the trees appear slightly more sparse here than they are just to the south.

49005.name: A Fork in the Road
49005.description: The path splits in three here. It leads southwest back towards the shattered gate and the entrance to the forest, but where it leads north and northeast is indiscernible, as the only things you can make out are more trees.

49006.name: Snow Covered Path
49006.description: The trees are less thick here, allowing more snow to drift in. Indeed, you now trudge through snow up to your knees. It would be a good idea to remain careful, as it isn't as easy to distinguish the path anymore. It appears to continue north and south, and you could likely plunge into the forest to the west.

49007.name: Snow Covered Path
49007.description: The snow remains quite heavy here, and a bit tiresome to travel through. The forest path is no longer clearly visible, however the trees are very thin to the north and south, suggesting the path likely lies in those directions under all this forsaken snow.

49008.name: Snow Covered Path
49008.description: The snow has blown over the path here, but not so bad as to make walking overly difficult. The fresh, undisturbed snow looks altogether beautiful here. It appears as though a small clearing lies not too distant to the north.

49009.name: A Well Tended Path
49009.description: The path here actually seems quite well tended. A thin sheet of fresh snow lies on the ground, but under it there seems to be no overgrowth. A small clearing and structure is visible off somewhere to the north.

49010.name: A Well Tended Path
49010.description: It almost seems a charming stroll as you walk this well maintained path. Snow crunches softly under your feed at you tread the easy road which continues east and west. Light forest lines the path to the north and south.

49011.name: An Overgrown Path
49011.description: The forestry is a bit thinner to the southwest, and most of the path seems reclaimed by nature. It would be wise to watch your step, as unseen hazards lie below the somewhat heavy snow. The path leads southwest and northeast, with trees crowding in along it.

49012.name: A Somewhat Smooth Path
49012.description: The road seems to be in better repair here, and there is little undergrowth to trouble about. A few divots mar the otherwise flat and smooth path which leads on southwest and northeast. It looks as though you'd have an easy enough time pushing into the forest surrounding the path.

49013.name: A Well Tended Path
49013.description: The path is in good shape here, despite the fact that the trees seem a bit more dense here than most other places along the road. Snow glitters all about you as the path heads southwest and northeast.

49014.name: A Fork in the Road
49014.description: The trees seem partially cleared here as three paths meet. They lead east, west, and southwest. The state of all the roads are in surprisingly good condition, and the snow coverage is light despite the fact that there are few trees to protect the roadway from drifts.

49015.name: A Cobblestone Path
49015.description: The path is actually paved here. Cobblestones peak through thin, glittering snow, and the familiar sound of stone echoes under your footfalls. The stones end to the west, but the paving continues to the east.

49016.name: A Cobblestone Path
49016.description: The snow falls lightly here, and lands on the cobblestone path, but oddly doesn't seem to accumulate atop it. The road leads west and bends northeast, and you can faintly hear the sound of rushing water coming from that direction.

49017.name: Before a Stone Bridge
49017.description: Just ahead of you to the east lies a small and unadorned stone bridge spanning a swiftly flowing river. The river itself doesn't seem too wide and you can make out the bank on the far side. The bridge itself however arches upward, obscuring what lies beyond it. The cobblestone path leads back to the southwest, and the trees thin out quite a bit here.

49018.name: A Fork in the Path
49018.description: The path splits, heading north, east, and south. The path continues on beyond sight to the east and south, but a small structure is discernable shortly to the north. The trees grow as thick as ever to the west, with no easy way to approach them readily apparent.

49019.name: Path's End
49019.description: The beaten path ends here, and what appears to be an old stone chapel lies shortly to the north. The forest surrounding the path seems much less dense here as well as the area immediately surrounding the chapel.

49020.name: Mouth of the Cave
49020.description: The mouth of the cave looms above you. Huge statues made of stone stand to either side of the opening. They look to be almost alive, seemingly guarding the entrance in an unending vigil. The inside of the cave is black. You can make out nothing past the first few feet.

49021.name: Intersection in the Tunnel
49021.description: The cavern is empty. It looks to be nothing more than a simple cross in the tunnel system. Tunnels lead away into the darkness in three directions. To the north is a smooth flat wall of stone. The only thing out of the ordinary is the huge gouges scratched into the floor, the mark of some unknown beast.

npcs.name: Vishnak
npcs.description: Vishnak is immense.  As he rises up before you, you wonder how anything living can be so large.  Especially something made of stone.  You can only imagine how much he must weigh.

49023.name: Dark Tunnel
49023.description: The tunnel curves away from where you stand so that you cannot see what lies at the other end. The walls are perfectly smooth, almost like glass. You notice the lack of stalagmites or stalactites, usually a common sight in a cave such as this.

49024.name: Dark Tunnel
49024.description: You can hear the sound of water dripping into a pool. It is hard to tell what direction the sound is coming from, though. It seems to echo off of the walls around you. You look down and see a few small puddles on the floor around your feet. The pool must be somewhere near.

49025.name: Underground Lake
49025.description: You have discovered the source of the dripping sound you heard. Water drips from stalactites on the ceiling into a great underground lake. The path ends here, the lake stretches away to the north and east into the darkness. The way west is still blocked by a stone wall that continues north bordering the lake.

49026.name: Sleeping Chamber
49026.description: The tunnel widens out into a large chamber. The two far corners are filled with straw. The piles have large depressions in them, as if some great weight once rested there but has been lifted. The piles have the look of beds, but you have to wonder what kind of creature is that huge.

49027.name: Dark Tunnel
49027.description: The ceiling here is covered in stalactites, the floor filled with stalagmites from the water dripping down. The air is damp and musky, the result of water gone stale from sitting underground for so many years. The straw tracked in from the north then left in the water does not help the smell any.

49028.name: Narrow Fissure
49028.description: The tunnel gets smaller here. It gets down to the point where you can barely squeeze through the crack. It widens a bit once you get past the center but still not enough to fit more than one person. Sucking in your chest and flattening against the wall, you begin to pull yourself through the fissure, your armor scraping noisily against the rock walls.

49029.name: Bone Room
49029.description: The stench of blood and death gags you. The ground is littered with the remains of the dragon's enemies and its meals, the walls have been stained red from the blood. Most are old and dry, but some of the remains still have flesh hanging onto the bones. A dragon skeleton fills one whole corner of the room, all that is left of the caves previous owner. Little else of interest is left, though you might find a few trinkets among the bones. Inside the dragon skeleton you see:

49030.name: Standing Before the Chapel
49030.description: You stand before a small stone building. It looks as if it has stood abandoned for many long years, and it has fallen into disrepair. The roof caved in toward the rear of the structure, taking parts of the rear and right walls with it. A sign remains over the door, though it hangs at an odd angle.

49031.name: Inside the Chapel
49031.description: The room is not large, it would only fit maybe twenty worshippers comfortably. There are strips of rug on the floor to kneel on, with a path down the center leading to a raised dais. Each side wall has one window in the middle and braziers holding torches for light. There is a large hole in the north wall that used to contain a stained glass window. Not enough remains to know what it depicted.

49032.name: On the Dais
49032.description: The remains of a statue stand in the center of the dais. It appears to have been damaged at the same time as the room and walls. The surface of the dais is covered in tiles. You see a few small pieces of stone on the ground that could be from the statue, but most of it appears to simply be gone.

49043.name: A Black Iron Gate
49043.description: A somewhat decorative gate of twisted black iron hinges from a sturdy stone wall about ten feet high. Inside the wall stretches a mass of ancient tombstones and monuments. The snow is piled high outside of the wall, yet inside only a thin sheet covers the ground, glistening like jewels and magnifying any light shone on it to a stunning effect.

npcs.name: Jim sinister revenant underwood
npcs.description: You make a dash for freedom. The trees close in around you.  They line the sides of the path so thick you cannot see more than a couple of feet to either side.  The path narrows a little here.  Branches spread out over your head, obscuring the sky from your view.  It continues on to the northwest and the southeast. You have lost 816 experience. You stand right near the path of the forest as it curves all about you, to the north, east, and west.  The evergreens grow in a bit thicker to the south. The cobblestones under your feet wobble as you walk along the path.  They aren't well tended and appear to have been abandoned for a long time.  The layer of snow over them helps to hide the coating of mud that has built up over the years.  You can see a fork in the road to the northeast. . . . Sangrhe the Redeemer glows with a strange light! The forest path curves around you to the east and south, a welcome sight. It is certainly better than trudging through the deep snow and trying to navigate through the countless trees which make it impossible to keep a straight line.  To the north and west, snow-heavy evergreens block your view for more than a few feet. The power of the ocean gradually subsides. The well kept forest path is visible just to the east, and it appears to stop before a tall gate built into a stone wall.  At least there is less snow to trudge through along the path. Surrounded by forest, you are grateful to be so close to the mountains.  To the north you can see them towering into the grey skies, serving as a landmark to help you keep your bearings.  Aside from that, everything looks exactly the same in every direction. (A lonely spirit wanders here despondently.) Breathing deeply, you run... Forest stretches in all directions, though you can faintly hear the sound of water off to the southeast.  The ground is a little uneven here, and every now and then the snow hides holes and small ledges in the ground.  A small inconvenience, but not at all pleasant when your whole leg sinks into the snow. . . . a winged valkyrie glows with a strange light! . . . a valkyrie glows with a strange light! Breathing deeply, you run... Surrounded by forest, you are grateful to be so close to the mountains.  To the north you can see them towering into the grey skies, serving as a landmark to help you keep your bearings.  Aside from that, everything looks exactly the same in every direction. (A lonely spirit wanders here despondently.) Breathing deeply, you run... To the west a river rages violently.  The water would likely be perfectly clear, having filtered through all the rocks to the north, but the strength of the waterfall creates churning white rapids.  You could probably push your boat into the river from here, but it isn't the safest of places to attempt such a thing. Breathing deeply, you run... There are few trees here, this close to the cold and jagged face of the northern mountain range.  To the west you can hear water crashing down from somewhere high in the rocks above.  To the east and south the forest extends still with a thin cover of evergreens. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. A very large wolf snarls threateningly at you. Breathing deeply, you run... The mountains block any progress to the north, which is just as well.  It must be barren wasteland any farther north than here.  In all other directions a few evergreens attempt a meager living, as far as plants go. Breathing deeply, you run... The rapids are still quite violent and strong here, not far from the waterfall.  The waters thrash you about, and you can only hope that you and your craft aren't dashed to pieces on the rocks or torn apart by the rapids themselves.  The immensely loud noise of the waterfall can be heard to the north, while the river continues to the south. The snow isn't nearly as thick on the ground here as in other places, and in fact you can see patches of dead grass between stretches of frost and thin sheets of ice.  The thin trees afford a view of a river off to the east, while the forest stretches on in all other directions. A short way away from the monstrous mountains to the north, the forest thrives a bit more here.  Many of the trees have died and fallen though, snow piled high on their trunks.  The forest continues to the west and to the south, and is still a bit thin to the east. The corpse of a slender daemon is lying here. The forest is quite thick here, and it is hard to see far in any direction. Progress is slow, as you are forced to weave your way through the myriad trees, trudging through the snow and watching for any fallen branches and trunks, and trying to avoid any near-invisible ice patches. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. A very large wolf snarls threateningly at you. The frost and thin, brittle yet slick ice make the footing a bit treacherous here.  You doubt falling would damage anything but your tailbone and your pride, but those are both things you'd like to keep intact.  The snow gives way to dead and dying grass eastward as the land slopes downward to a slowly flowing river.  Countless trees stretch out in all other directions. The forest is dead quiet except for the sound of rushing water off to the east.  A thin layer of snow crunches under your feet, each footprint revealing frozen, dead grass beneath. The bridge itself however arches upward, obscuring what lies beyond it.  The cobblestone path leads back to the southwest, and the trees thin out quite a bit here. You sense someone hidden in the room. Your only options for travel here are the thick forest to the north and west, as to the south and east lies the mysterious giant crater.  Idly you wonder how many dead, crushed trees are buried under the snow that settled since the massive crater was formed. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering. You stand at the brink of a gigantic impact crater.  Minding your step near the edge, you gaze down to discern what appears to be a large meteor in the center.  The crater is so large that you cannot further explore it.  If you managed to survive the fall down, you likely wouldn't be able to find a way back out. To the north and east off in the distance you can hear the distinctive sound of a rushing river.  As you peer through the trees to the south and west, it seems as though they thin out considerably, instead of growing in thicker as you would expect. Standing on the eastern bank of the peaceful river, you take a moment to admire the beautiful waters.  The trees are actually a few yards behind you as you face the river, and are quite thin back there.  The river bends along to the north and east, while the forest stretches out to the south and west. A snowshoe hare hops about aimlessly. A large, lithe white leopard prowls the area. The forest is a goodly distance to the east and west of the banks here. Perhaps the trees had been cleared out a bit to more clearly mark the path across the river.  In any case, the water is certainly calm enough for you to make your way ashore and follow the nearby pathways that extend east and west from the simple stone bridge to the north.  The river itself bends gently to the east. A bit dark and altogether tranquil is the area under the stone bridge.  It is slightly cooler here than on the rest of the river, but not too uncomfortably so.  You notice smooth rocks piled along the banks.  The bridge is in perfect condition, so apparently the builders of the bridge simply dumped excess stones into the river instead of carting them off somewhere for disposal. As the rapids calm, small blocks of ice begin to float about, but not nearly enough to slow your progress down the river.  The forest begins to recede further to the east and west of the river, and just to the south a small stone bridge spanning the water is visible. The river is now quite tranquil and navigable.  You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty.
