Breathing deeply, you run... The path splits off in three directions here.  To the southwest the path heads back to the river.  A large gate guards the only visible opening in a walled off area.  The southeastern path continues into the trees toward some unknown destination. A somewhat decorative gate of twisted black iron hinges from a sturdy stone wall about ten feet high.  Inside the wall stretches a mass of ancient tombstones and monuments.  The snow is piled high outside of the wall, yet inside only a thin sheet covers the ground, glistening like jewels and magnifying any light shone on it to a stunning effect. Ok. Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. This portion of the graveyard is filled with graves that were dug long ago but never used.  Very few tombstones dot the land, and the ones that are here have begun to deteriorate.  A few are toppled over, many are missing chunks from the edges, and most bear at least some other light marring of their form. You begin to hear voices in your head, as if the graves cry out to be filled... by you.  In a brief fit of insanity, you see a marker over an empty grave which bears your name. You take 3 drinks of water from an enchanted, leather drinking flask. Face ¯ ~a mask of clear chitin~ Back ¯ ~the pack of the seasoned adventurer~ Wrist ¯ ~a bangle of dwarven steel~ Wrist ¯ ~a glittering bracelet of silver~ Hands ¯ ~a pair of white tiger claws~ Finger ¯ ~the Ring of the Alpha~ Waist ¯ ~a glimmering belt of shinerock~ (lit) Feet ¯ ~a pair of crimson wyrmskin boots~ Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. Decay runs rampant inside this old stone structure.  A few tombs stand at the walls about four feet high.  Upon each is a worn down carving bearing holy symbols and the same family crest.  As you walk about the room, loose chunks of the stone floor crunch under feet.  Off in the northeast corner, a large hole leads down into darkness.  Strangely, it looks as if something with incredible strength burst upwards through the stone floor from below, and claw marks etched deep into stone surround the hole. Fear grips you instantly, and you press yourself against the nearest wall, glancing about in the darkness frantically.  You find yourself in a very small and cramped tunnel which leads to the south.  It is hard to breathe, and you begin to feel as though you're suffocating. The tunnel opens up into an expansive area with a rather low ceiling.  Dirt walls stand in all directions save east, in which the area slopes gently downward into darkness.  This underground passage is much warmer than the forest above.  You begin to break out into a sweat but are unable to tell if it is because it is unnaturally hot down here, or if it just feels that way because your body has grown accustomed to the chill of the forest. You tumble east. You gently float downward and come to a stop. You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones.  As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them.  The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses.  How singularly odd. . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood glows with a strange light! . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood looks like he might have something to say. Mostly bone with gruesome bits of flesh and muscle still clinging to his skeleton, he fixes his one eye on you and begins to amble in your direction.

49044.name: A Large Graveyard
49044.description: Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them. There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off. One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar. The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene.

The rapids are still quite violent and strong here, not far from the waterfall. A short way away from the monstrous mountains to the north, the forest thrives a bit more here.  Many of the trees have died and fallen though, snow piled high on their trunks. The forest is quite thick here, and it is hard to see far in any direction. Progress is slow, as you are forced to weave your way through the myriad trees, trudging through the snow and watching for any fallen branches and trunks, and trying to avoid any near-invisible ice patches. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. A very large wolf snarls threateningly at you. The frost and thin, brittle yet slick ice make the footing a bit treacherous here.  You doubt falling would damage anything but your tailbone and your pride, but those are both things you'd like to keep intact. To the north and east off in the distance you can hear the distinctive sound of a rushing river.  As you peer through the trees to the south and west, it seems as though they thin out considerably, instead of growing in thicker as you would expect. Standing on the eastern bank of the peaceful river, you take a moment to admire the beautiful waters.  The trees are actually a few yards behind you as you face the river, and are quite thin back there.  The river bends along to the north and east, while the forest stretches out to the south and west. A snowshoe hare hops about aimlessly. A large, lithe white leopard prowls the area. The forest is a goodly distance to the east and west of the banks here. Perhaps the trees had been cleared out a bit to more clearly mark the path across the river. As the rapids calm, small blocks of ice begin to float about, but not nearly enough to slow your progress down the river. Breathing deeply, you run... The path splits off in three directions here.  To the southwest the path heads back to the river.  A large gate guards the only visible opening in a walled off area.  The southeastern path continues into the trees toward some unknown destination. A somewhat decorative gate of twisted black iron hinges from a sturdy stone wall about ten feet high.  Inside the wall stretches a mass of ancient tombstones and monuments.  The snow is piled high outside of the wall, yet inside only a thin sheet covers the ground, glistening like jewels and magnifying any light shone on it to a stunning effect. Ok. Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. This portion of the graveyard is filled with graves that were dug long ago but never used.  Very few tombstones dot the land, and the ones that are here have begun to deteriorate.  A few are toppled over, many are missing chunks from the edges, and most bear at least some other light marring of their form. You begin to hear voices in your head, as if the graves cry out to be filled... by you.  In a brief fit of insanity, you see a marker over an empty grave which bears your name. The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. Decay runs rampant inside this old stone structure.  A few tombs stand at the walls about four feet high.  Upon each is a worn down carving bearing holy symbols and the same family crest.  As you walk about the room, loose chunks of the stone floor crunch under feet.  Off in the northeast corner, a large hole leads down into darkness.  Strangely, it looks as if something with incredible strength burst upwards through the stone floor from below, and claw marks etched deep into stone surround the hole. Fear grips you instantly, and you press yourself against the nearest wall, glancing about in the darkness frantically.  You find yourself in a very small and cramped tunnel which leads to the south.  It is hard to breathe, and you begin to feel as though you're suffocating. The tunnel opens up into an expansive area with a rather low ceiling.  Dirt walls stand in all directions save east, in which the area slopes gently downward into darkness.  This underground passage is much warmer than the forest above.  You begin to break out into a sweat but are unable to tell if it is because it is unnaturally hot down here, or if it just feels that way because your body has grown accustomed to the chill of the forest. You tumble east. You gently float downward and come to a stop. You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones.  As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them.  The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses.  How singularly odd. . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood glows with a strange light! . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood looks like he might have something to say. Mostly bone with gruesome bits of flesh and muscle still clinging to his skeleton, he fixes his one eye on you and begins to amble in your direction.

To the north and east off in the distance you can hear the distinctive sound of a rushing river.  As you peer through the trees to the south and west, it seems as though they thin out considerably, instead of growing in thicker as you would expect. Perhaps the trees had been cleared out a bit to more clearly mark the path across the river. The bridge is in perfect condition, so apparently the builders of the bridge simply dumped excess stones into the river instead of carting them off somewhere for disposal. As the rapids calm, small blocks of ice begin to float about, but not nearly enough to slow your progress down the river. Breathing deeply, you run... The path splits off in three directions here.  To the southwest the path heads back to the river.  A large gate guards the only visible opening in a walled off area.  The southeastern path continues into the trees toward some unknown destination. A somewhat decorative gate of twisted black iron hinges from a sturdy stone wall about ten feet high.  Inside the wall stretches a mass of ancient tombstones and monuments.  The snow is piled high outside of the wall, yet inside only a thin sheet covers the ground, glistening like jewels and magnifying any light shone on it to a stunning effect. Ok. Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. This portion of the graveyard is filled with graves that were dug long ago but never used.  Very few tombstones dot the land, and the ones that are here have begun to deteriorate.  A few are toppled over, many are missing chunks from the edges, and most bear at least some other light marring of their form. You begin to hear voices in your head, as if the graves cry out to be filled... by you.  In a brief fit of insanity, you see a marker over an empty grave which bears your name. You take 3 drinks of water from an enchanted, leather drinking flask. There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. Decay runs rampant inside this old stone structure.  A few tombs stand at the walls about four feet high.  Upon each is a worn down carving bearing holy symbols and the same family crest.  As you walk about the room, loose chunks of the stone floor crunch under feet.  Off in the northeast corner, a large hole leads down into darkness.  Strangely, it looks as if something with incredible strength burst upwards through the stone floor from below, and claw marks etched deep into stone surround the hole. Fear grips you instantly, and you press yourself against the nearest wall, glancing about in the darkness frantically.  You find yourself in a very small and cramped tunnel which leads to the south.  It is hard to breathe, and you begin to feel as though you're suffocating. The tunnel opens up into an expansive area with a rather low ceiling.  Dirt walls stand in all directions save east, in which the area slopes gently downward into darkness.  This underground passage is much warmer than the forest above.  You begin to break out into a sweat but are unable to tell if it is because it is unnaturally hot down here, or if it just feels that way because your body has grown accustomed to the chill of the forest. You tumble east. You gently float downward and come to a stop. You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones.  As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them.  The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses.  How singularly odd. . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood glows with a strange light! . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood looks like he might have something to say. Mostly bone with gruesome bits of flesh and muscle still clinging to his skeleton, he fixes his one eye on you and begins to amble in your direction.

49048.name: Pit of Bones
49048.description: You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones. As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them. The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses. How singularly odd.

The path narrows a little here.  Branches spread out over your head, obscuring the sky from your view.  It continues on to the northwest and the southeast. You have lost 816 experience. You stand right near the path of the forest as it curves all about you, to the north, east, and west.  The evergreens grow in a bit thicker to the south. The cobblestones under your feet wobble as you walk along the path.  They aren't well tended and appear to have been abandoned for a long time.  The layer of snow over them helps to hide the coating of mud that has built up over the years.  You can see a fork in the road to the northeast. . . . Sangrhe the Redeemer glows with a strange light! The forest path curves around you to the east and south, a welcome sight. It is certainly better than trudging through the deep snow and trying to navigate through the countless trees which make it impossible to keep a straight line. The well kept forest path is visible just to the east, and it appears to stop before a tall gate built into a stone wall.  At least there is less snow to trudge through along the path. Surrounded by forest, you are grateful to be so close to the mountains.  To the north you can see them towering into the grey skies, serving as a landmark to help you keep your bearings. The trees continue to the south and east preventing you from observing your surroundings for more than a few feet. The trees are a bit thin here, a small distance away from the forest path. Looking about, you're afforded a view of naught but trees in all directions. They all look pretty much the same.  Try not to get lost. . . . a winged valkyrie glows with a strange light! . . . a valkyrie glows with a strange light! You sense someone hidden in the room. The sound of snow crunching under your feet has begun to sound like some sort of mantra, rhythmic and soft.  To the east, a blotch of blackness obstructs your view as the branches of countless trees wind together.  The forest appears relatively inviting in all other directions. Your only options for travel here are the thick forest to the north and west, as to the south and east lies the mysterious giant crater.  Idly you wonder how many dead, crushed trees are buried under the snow that settled since the massive crater was formed. Crystalline fronds of frostfern spread across the frozen ground, glittering. The sound of snow crunching under your feet has begun to sound like some sort of mantra, rhythmic and soft.  To the east, a blotch of blackness obstructs your view as the branches of countless trees wind together.  The forest appears relatively inviting in all other directions. You stand at the brink of a gigantic impact crater.  Minding your step near the edge, you gaze down to discern what appears to be a large meteor in the center.  The crater is so large that you cannot further explore it.  If you managed to survive the fall down, you likely wouldn't be able to find a way back out. To the north and east off in the distance you can hear the distinctive sound of a rushing river.  As you peer through the trees to the south and west, it seems as though they thin out considerably, instead of growing in thicker as you would expect. Standing on the eastern bank of the peaceful river, you take a moment to admire the beautiful waters.  The trees are actually a few yards behind you as you face the river, and are quite thin back there.  The river bends along to the north and east, while the forest stretches out to the south and west. A snowshoe hare hops about aimlessly. A large, lithe white leopard prowls the area. The forest is a goodly distance to the east and west of the banks here. Perhaps the trees had been cleared out a bit to more clearly mark the path across the river.  In any case, the water is certainly calm enough for you to make your way ashore and follow the nearby pathways that extend east and west from the simple stone bridge to the north.  The river itself bends gently to the east. A bit dark and altogether tranquil is the area under the stone bridge.  It is slightly cooler here than on the rest of the river, but not too uncomfortably so.  You notice smooth rocks piled along the banks.  The bridge is in perfect condition, so apparently the builders of the bridge simply dumped excess stones into the river instead of carting them off somewhere for disposal. As the rapids calm, small blocks of ice begin to float about, but not nearly enough to slow your progress down the river.  The forest begins to recede further to the east and west of the river, and just to the south a small stone bridge spanning the water is visible. The river is now quite tranquil and navigable.  You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty.  The curse of this forest bears heavily on you, and you can't shake the thought that any moment here could be your last. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light. To the west, the river courses gently downstream.  It would be quite a peaceful boat ride and a brief escape from snow and trees.  The forest path to the east and south offers similar relief and the chance at a gentle stroll, as opposed to a straining trudge. . . . a winged valkyrie glows with a strange light! . . . a valkyrie glows with a strange light! A snowshoe hare hops about aimlessly. A huge white bear lumbers through the snow. Breathing deeply, you run... The river is now quite tranquil and navigable.  You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty.  The curse of this forest bears heavily on you, and you can't shake the thought that any moment here could be your last. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light. The river is just now beginning to calm, a tad bit downstream from the waterfall, though it is still quite swift.  The forest is visible not too far off from the banks of the river to the east and west, and you could easily make your way to dry land and head through the forest.  The river begins to bend slightly eastward as it continues downstream. The river is now quite tranquil and navigable.  You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty.  The curse of this forest bears heavily on you, and you can't shake the thought that any moment here could be your last. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light. You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here.  It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north.  It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest. The river is now quite tranquil and navigable.  You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty.  The curse of this forest bears heavily on you, and you can't shake the thought that any moment here could be your last. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light. You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here.  It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north.  It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest. To the west a river rages violently.  The water would likely be perfectly clear, having filtered through all the rocks to the north, but the strength of the waterfall creates churning white rapids.  You could probably push your boat into the river from here, but it isn't the safest of places to attempt such a thing. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. An arctic wolf trails behind the leader. A very large wolf snarls threateningly at you. You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here.  It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north.  It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest. Breathing deeply, you run... Forest stretches in all directions, though you can faintly hear the sound of water off to the southeast.  The ground is a little uneven here, and every now and then the snow hides holes and small ledges in the ground.  A small inconvenience, but not at all pleasant when your whole leg sinks into the snow. You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here.  It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north.  It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest. Breathing deeply, you run... The river is just now beginning to calm, a tad bit downstream from the waterfall, though it is still quite swift.  The forest is visible not too far off from the banks of the river to the east and west, and you could easily make your way to dry land and head through the forest.  The river begins to bend slightly eastward as it continues downstream. You stand at a bend in the river as it curves along to the south and east here.  It seems to have calmed down here, a short distance from the mighty waterfall to the north.  It would be very easy to push a boat in and at least give your tired and cold feet a rest. Breathing deeply, you run... Forest stretches in all directions, though you can faintly hear the sound of water off to the southeast.  The ground is a little uneven here, and every now and then the snow hides holes and small ledges in the ground.  A small inconvenience, but not at all pleasant when your whole leg sinks into the snow. Breathing deeply, you run... The forest path curves around you to the east and south, a welcome sight. It is certainly better than trudging through the deep snow and trying to navigate through the countless trees which make it impossible to keep a straight line.  To the north and west, snow-heavy evergreens block your view for more than a few feet. (A lonely spirit wanders here despondently.) (A lonely spirit wanders here despondently.) Breathing deeply, you run... The path splits off in three directions here.  To the southwest the path heads back to the river.  A large gate guards the only visible opening in a walled off area.  The southeastern path continues into the trees toward some unknown destination. A somewhat decorative gate of twisted black iron hinges from a sturdy stone wall about ten feet high.  Inside the wall stretches a mass of ancient tombstones and monuments.  The snow is piled high outside of the wall, yet inside only a thin sheet covers the ground, glistening like jewels and magnifying any light shone on it to a stunning effect. Ok. Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. This portion of the graveyard is filled with graves that were dug long ago but never used.  Very few tombstones dot the land, and the ones that are here have begun to deteriorate.  A few are toppled over, many are missing chunks from the edges, and most bear at least some other light marring of their form. You begin to hear voices in your head, as if the graves cry out to be filled... by you.  In a brief fit of insanity, you see a marker over an empty grave which bears your name. You take 3 drinks of water from an enchanted, leather drinking flask. Face ¯ ~a mask of clear chitin~ Back ¯ ~the pack of the seasoned adventurer~ Wrist ¯ ~a bangle of dwarven steel~ Wrist ¯ ~a glittering bracelet of silver~ Hands ¯ ~a pair of white tiger claws~ Finger ¯ ~the Ring of the Alpha~ Waist ¯ ~a glimmering belt of shinerock~ (lit) Feet ¯ ~a pair of crimson wyrmskin boots~ Quite a number of graves are here - some marked by the simplest of stones while others bear large, masterfully carved statues - with simple cobblestone pathways leading around and to them.  There are also a number of mausoleums with high arched entryways and a few tombs that have been completely sealed off.  One of the mausoleums seems particularly old, and it's stone door stands ajar.  The graveyard seems quite peaceful and serene. Decay runs rampant inside this old stone structure.  A few tombs stand at the walls about four feet high.  Upon each is a worn down carving bearing holy symbols and the same family crest.  As you walk about the room, loose chunks of the stone floor crunch under feet.  Off in the northeast corner, a large hole leads down into darkness.  Strangely, it looks as if something with incredible strength burst upwards through the stone floor from below, and claw marks etched deep into stone surround the hole. Fear grips you instantly, and you press yourself against the nearest wall, glancing about in the darkness frantically.  You find yourself in a very small and cramped tunnel which leads to the south.  It is hard to breathe, and you begin to feel as though you're suffocating. The tunnel opens up into an expansive area with a rather low ceiling.  Dirt walls stand in all directions save east, in which the area slopes gently downward into darkness.  This underground passage is much warmer than the forest above.  You begin to break out into a sweat but are unable to tell if it is because it is unnaturally hot down here, or if it just feels that way because your body has grown accustomed to the chill of the forest. You tumble east. You gently float downward and come to a stop. You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones.  As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them.  The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses.  How singularly odd. . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood glows with a strange light! . . . Jim sinister revenant underwood looks like he might have something to say. Mostly bone with gruesome bits of flesh and muscle still clinging to his skeleton, he fixes his one eye on you and begins to amble in your direction.

49049.name: Pit of Bones
49049.description: You struggle to stand on a floor made entirely of human bones. As you thrash about, bones crunch and crack under your feet, making quite the ruckus. All of the bones have long since lost any flesh attached to them. The walls and ceiling are made of stone, as if this room was designed for the specific purpose it is being put to - namely holding thousands of human corpses. How singularly odd.

49050.name: A Bridge Over the River
49050.description: You stand on an unadorned bridge made of smooth stone over the river. The water churns below your feet, rushing over rocks and spraying a fine mist into the air. You shiver as some of the water contacts with your flesh - it is almost frozen.

49051.name: Before a Stone Bridge
49051.description: The trees are less numerous along the bank of the river than elsewhere in the forest. A stone bridge spans the river to the southwest and the path continues on the other side. The bank in the surrounding area appears to be too steep to get down to the water.

49052.name: A Cobblestone Path
49052.description: The cobblestones under your feet wobble as you walk along the path. They aren't well tended and appear to have been abandoned for a long time. The layer of snow over them helps to hide the coating of mud that has built up over the years. You can see a fork in the road to the northeast.

49053.name: A Fork in the Road
49053.description: The path splits off in three directions here. To the southwest the path heads back to the river. A large gate guards the only visible opening in a walled off area. The southeastern path continues into the trees toward some unknown destination.

49054.name: A Cobblestone Path
49054.description: The trees close in around you. They line the sides of the path so thick you cannot see more than a couple of feet to either side. The path narrows a little here. Branches spread out over your head, obscuring the sky from your view. It continues on to the northwest and the southeast.

49055.name: A Cobblestone Path
49055.description: You stand in a curve in the path. The cobblestones stretch away to the northwest, and into the distance to the east. Far to the east you can just make out what looks to be the roof of a building. The trees are slightly thinner here. It looks as if you might be able to push your way through.

49056.name: A Cobblestone Path
49056.description: Dirt path meets cobblestones under your feet. The stone road looks as if perhaps it was supposed to go further, but was never completed. The soft glow of lantern light bathes you from the large building visible to the east. The path leads right to the front door.

49060.name: The Dormitory Door
49060.description: You stand before a great double door adorned by large bronze knockers. Weathered lanterns hang from hooks on either side. They contain no oil, but continue burning anyway. You can see more lights through various windows visible from where you stand. The trees stop about ten feet away from the building.

49061.name: A Large Entryway
49061.description: You stand in the entryway to the building. A set of stairs set off to the side lead upward to the second floor. Further inside the building you see the main lobby. The area is well-lit by lanterns hanging at intervals on the wall, though several of them have long since burned out.

49062.name: The Common Room
49062.description: This is where the inhabitants gathered during their free time. There are two long couches and several chairs spread throughout the room. Several tables are set up with various games that you do not recognize. A large window shows you a view of an overgrown, open air garden in the center of the building.

49063.name: Northern Hallway
49063.description: The hallway is unadorned save for the lanterns set on the walls to light the way. Doors are located at even intervals, leading into the rooms of the people that used to live here. All but one appear to be locked or stuck shut. A path has been worn into the floor from many years of use.

49064.name: Northern Hallway
49064.description: The hallway appears to be quite run-down. A half-broken door to the north gives you a view of the sky through a hole in the wall and ceiling of the room beyond. The remaining part of the door appears jammed shut.

49065.name: Northern Hallway
49065.description: The lights in this section of hallway stopped working long ago, leaving the hallway dark. Chairs lie abandoned along the wall outside of one of the rooms, and the walls are stained from long years of water leaking down through the roof. An angry spirit grins with malice and swings its fist into your neck. (23) Immunity! You drift forward and harass an angry spirit. (2) A sudden, fierce gust of air surrounds you, propelling your weapons back towards an angry spirit! Well aimed! Immunity! Trembling, you bump an angry spirit with your dagger. (3) The ocean's energy begins to flow through you, granting you the speed of a gentle current.

49066.name: Northern Hallway
49066.description: The walls here are splattered with blood. It appears to have originated from the room to the north. Although you are no expert in such things, you think the blood is human. There is also some white dust in a pile on the floor. You have a sneaking suspicion that you know where it came from...

49067.name: Southern Hallway
49067.description: A simple green carpet runs down the length of the hall, looking surprisingly new for how long it must have been here. Doors to rooms lie on each side of the hall at even intervals, most of them jammed shut and resisting your strongest efforts at forcing them open. An unnatural, haunting silence begins to pervade your mind.

49068.name: Southern Hallway
49068.description: The hallway looks much like the rest of the hallways in the dormitory. The green carpet, now tattered and ripped in places, ends at a set of large wooden double doors to the east. As you approach the set of doors, you can just make out the sound of voices coming from the other side...

49069.name: Southern Hallway
49069.description: A somewhat large alcove lies in the west side of the hallway here. Cushioned benches line the wall of the alcove under massive windows, broken in many places and frozen over with snow and ice where still in-tact. The rest of the hallway seems to be in relatively good condition.

49070.name: Southern Hallway
49070.description: The hallway bends to the east here. A small red rug with gold tassels lies before a large set of double doors to the southwest. A few comfortable looking chairs sit near the walls, often with abandoned books and writing desks scattered haphazardly around them. A simple green carpet begins leading down the eastward hallway.

49071.name: Study
49071.description: You stand in a rather large study. The walls are lined with bookcases containing volumes commonly found in most intellectual institutions of Ishar. Softwood tables made of pine stand about the room with simple chairs at them. A number of texts still lie open upon the tables. The windows are mostly broken, shattered from the cold, and individual pieces of paper litter the floor. A cold breeze drifts through the eerie silence.

49072.name: An Empty Room
49072.description: The room shows no signs of ever having been occupied. It is completely empty, devoid of any furniture. There are not even marks on the floor showing where furniture used to lie. The windows are completely boarded over, and the floor is covered in centuries of dust.

49073.name: A Dormitory Room
49073.description: It appears as if the last occupant left in a hurry. The bed is unmade, and half of the dresser's drawers are open with rotting clothes strewn about. The open closet door reveals several completely empty hangers, the rest holding mere scraps of the garments left there.

49074.name: A Dormitory Room
49074.description: You stand in the middle of a typical room. It could still be lived in by the looks of things, other than the layer of dust coating everything. There are a few items lying on the floor forgotten, the bed is a bit rumpled, and one of the dresser drawers is partially open.

49075.name: A Dormitory Room
49075.description: The first thing you notice is the painting covering the entire back wall, working around the window. It expands partway onto the side walls as well, but those portions were never completed. All of the furniture has been moved around so that the entire area was accessible.

49076.name: A Grisly Scene of Death
49076.description: You barely restrain yourself from throwing up as you take in the scene. Most of the floor is stained with blood. You see writing on the wall, also blood. About half of a skeleton lies near the wall beneath the writing. Other pieces are strewn about the room. Still more bones appear to have been ground into dust, apparently while the victim was still living.

49077.name: A Dormitory Room
49077.description: You do not see anything out of place in the room. The closet doors are shut and locked. The bed is made, the desk is organized. The floor is completely picked up and there is not a speck of dust to be seen. You have no idea how they managed that last part with as long as the room has been unoccupied...

49084.name: The Dining Room
49084.description: The stench of old blood fills your nostrils. This room witnessed a truly brutal massacre. Something apparently drove the men here insane with anger, forcing them to turn on one another. They slaughtered each other in their rage, using their utensils as their instruments of death. The violence of it causes your head to spin with nausea.

49085.name: The Kitchen
49085.description: The brutal violence of the dining room apparently extended over into the kitchen as well. It is almost worse here, as the kitchen has many more deadly weapons than simple forks and dull knives. The kitchen staff used everything at their disposal, from extremely sharp knives and cleavers, to heavy pots and dishes.

49087.name: An Open-Air Garden
49087.description: While the garden might once have been a place of exquisite beauty, it has fallen into disrepair with no one to care for it. Weeds have killed almost all of the flowers, and one of the large trees has grown out enough to block out the sun for many other plants, leaving several patches of dirt where those plants died out.

49088.name: An Outcropping on the Cliff
49088.description: You come out onto an outcropping of rock about two thirds of the way up the face of the cliff. The height gives you a magnificent view of the entire valley and the white forest, a sight which takes your breath away. Unfortunately the only way you see to get back to the ground is to jump...

49091.name: The White Forest
49091.description: The trees are suddenly less thick here. Many are small and stunted. One tree is much larger than the rest, flourishing while its neighbors manage with scrap nutrients. Snow continues to drift from overhead, and the forest stretches out all around you.

49092.name: Inside the Hollow Tree
49092.description: A small amount of snow has drifted in from the hole above, but most of the ground is dirt. Tangled branches and dirt walls surround you in this cramped space. Luckily, the hole to get back to the world outside lies just in reach.

49093.name: A Cramped Tunnel
49093.description: While a bit cramped, this tunnel is just wide enough to squeeze through. The branches crowd the southern end of the tunnel.

49094.name: The Sleeper's Chamber
49094.description: A stone slab somewhat resembling a bed sits in the center of this surprisingly large chamber. The walls, floor, and ceiling are all dirt, but shaped in a way to almost resemble the room of a house. Torches affixed to the walls allow a low and ambient light.

49095.name: A Rocky Tunnel
49095.description: The passageway is just wide enough for you to squeeze and crawl your way through. Sharp broken rocks stab and scratch at you with every inch of progress you make. You silently pray that the tons of rock above you don't suddenly cave in again, instantly killing you and entombing you forever in these accursed mountains. No exits are apparent.

49096.name: Just Beyond the Cave-In
49096.description: The area here is still forested, though unexplainably warm. The snow under your feet is slush, and has melted away revealing thick green grass a few yards out from the cave in. You see all around you more forest, and in the distance towering mountains. This area doesn't seem to promise a way out of the forest. The cave-in lies immediately to the west.

49097.name: In the Forest
49097.description: The forest seems to be the thickest here, and significantly fewer trees lie in all directions from where you are standing. Although not everything is coated in snow and ice, a feeling of silence and utter solitude is still noticeable.

49098.name: The Thin Forest
49098.description: The trees are quite thin here. To the west, mountains loom in the near distance, and it seems the trees give way to a vast grassland to the east. The forest is much thicker to the south.

49099.name: The Thin Forest
49099.description: You have made it away from frozen forest and yet still no signs of natural life, animal or human, spoil the forest. No chirping birds, no buzzing bugs, no pitter-patter of tiny woodland animal feet. The only resident seems to be the wind, and it is but fleeting.

49100.name: Along the Mountain Face
49100.description: You stand before the wall of the mountains to your east. The trees have thinned out, as the terrain has become too rocky for them to grow. Any hopes you had of escaping through here have collapsed - the mountain is completely impassible.

49101.name: Along the Mountain Face
49101.description: The mountains loom before you to the north, barring the way to any more frozen wastelands - what a loss. Like the rest of the mountain range, they are unscalable, confining you to the bowl of the mountains.

49102.name: Along the Mountain Face
49102.description: In the distance you can sometimes hear loose rock tumbling from an incredibly high altitude. The cave-in area lies shortly to the south. The forest seems pretty thin and navigable to the east, and while the rocky ground is nothing spectacular, is a nice change of scenery from all the dirt and underbrush.

49103.name: A Vast Grassland
49103.description: There are no signs that trees ever grew at all in this area. The flat ground stretches in all directions, covered with incredibly thick and lush green grass. The grass is only an inch or so high, but shows no signs of ever being disturbed by any living organism.

49104.name: A Field of Wildflowers
49104.description: Incredible! You stand in an unbelievably vast field of wildflowers. Nearly every type of flower you have ever seen, and a great deal many you have not, grow in abundance here. The colors are breathtaking. Every step you take crushes multiple blossoms beneath your feet. Looking about, it is clear that yours are not the only feet to have trampled these wildflowers. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light. A tall stand of valerian sways gently, its pink flowers releasing a pungent odor.

npcs.name: Living statue
npcs.description: It is a marble statue of a man, but alive!  Slightly tall, he is depicted as wearing a light suit of armor.  His face is expressionless though he seems to be crying.

49105.name: Edge of the Forest
49105.description: The forest suddenly ends here, all the trees stopping in a straight line. The land seems to become progressively desolate away from the forest, chiefly rocks and very dry dirt. In some places, short yellowed grass manages to grow in small patches. To your east and west, the scenery seems to remain the same until it meets the impassible mountains, yet to the south some sort of structure is just visible.

49106.name: A Broken Gate
49106.description: You stand at a rickety old wooden gate. It is in a state of serious disrepair, and is broken in more places than it is in-tact. Most of the wood is rotting and many logs have become the home of boring bugs. An old house lies to the south, and the gate encloses the house's pitiful yard.

49107.name: A Dilapidated House
49107.description: The old house looks even worse on the inside. It is completely bare of any ornamentation. Only a few tables and chairs occupy the singular room, and they have long since been smashed to bits. A couple of empty bookcases stand at the walls. There is a second story, but the staircase has been destroyed, making access impossible. A small trapdoor lies in the corner.

49109.name: Beside an Old House
49109.description: You stand in the yard next to the rundown old house. It is quite barren, and in actuality no grass grows here. The ground is far too rocky and dry to support any plant life. Not to mention the owner didn't seem to care much about the condition of his property. To the west, the old house stands, mostly falling apart.

49110.name: Backyard
49110.description: Scraggly yellow grass grows in patches in the backyard of the old house. A tool shed stands out in one corner of the yard, but the door has long since rusted shut. The wooden fence continues to circle yard, and while you could easily scale it, beyond there is nothing but more desolation and the face of the mountains.

49111.name: A White Waterfall
49111.description: A breathtaking waterfall spills perfectly pure white water down into a river about twenty yards across. The water cascades off shelves of rocks before crashing into the river and churning violently, stopping any ice from forming at all despite the cold temperatures. The mountains to the north are unscalable, but the river continues to the south. Deep blue mineral veins pulse faintly with color.

49112.name: The Strong Rapids
49112.description: The rapids are still quite violent and strong here, not far from the waterfall. The waters thrash you about, and you can only hope that you and your craft aren't dashed to pieces on the rocks or torn apart by the rapids themselves. The immensely loud noise of the waterfall can be heard to the north, while the river continues to the south.

49113.name: Gentle Rapids
49113.description: The river is just now beginning to calm, a tad bit downstream from the waterfall, though it is still quite swift. The forest is visible not too far off from the banks of the river to the east and west, and you could easily make your way to dry land and head through the forest. The river begins to bend slightly eastward as it continues downstream.

49114.name: Along a Tranquil River
49114.description: The river is now quite tranquil and navigable. You take a moment to lift an eye to the heavens and watch the sky drift by overhead, conscious that this may be the last time you have the chance to observe its beauty. The curse of this forest bears heavily on you, and you can't shake the thought that any moment here could be your last. Pure white blossoms grow here, radiating a soft divine light.

49115.name: Along a Tranquil River
49115.description: As the rapids calm, small blocks of ice begin to float about, but not nearly enough to slow your progress down the river. The forest begins to recede further to the east and west of the river, and just to the south a small stone bridge spanning the water is visible.

49116.name: Under the Bridge
49116.description: A bit dark and altogether tranquil is the area under the stone bridge. It is slightly cooler here than on the rest of the river, but not too uncomfortably so. You notice smooth rocks piled along the banks. The bridge is in perfect condition, so apparently the builders of the bridge simply dumped excess stones into the river instead of carting them off somewhere for disposal.

49117.name: Along a Tranquil River
49117.description: The forest is a goodly distance to the east and west of the banks here. Perhaps the trees had been cleared out a bit to more clearly mark the path across the river. In any case, the water is certainly calm enough for you to make your way ashore and follow the nearby pathways that extend east and west from the simple stone bridge to the north. The river itself bends gently to the east.

49118.name: A Bend in the River
49118.description: The river bends more eastward here, picking up a little bit of speed as it rounds the easy curve. Foam sprays at you as the water crashes against rocks, the banks, and your boat. You involuntarily shiver at the ice-cold sensation against your skin.

49119.name: Along a Tranquil River
49119.description: The river begins to bend slightly more southward again here, and slows down from the pace it assumed in the harder curve upstream. As it begins to slow, little blocks of ice begin to float around. The river seems almost determined to not completely freeze over, as if it has a will of its own.

49120.name: Along a Tranquil River
49120.description: The forest begins to grow in closer to the river here. In fact, it is so close that in some places low branches begin to reach into the river. A thin layer of snow collects atop each of these small branches, and the river is more than calm enough for you to admire the majestic beauty of the snow and frost covered trees as you pass them.

49121.name: Along a Tranquil River
49121.description: Here a few trees have actually died and fallen into the river. Luckily the river is slightly wider here, and so navigating around the fallen trees isn't impossible but merely a tiresome nuisance. The water crashes into these dead logs, spraying you with cold water despite your best efforts to remain dry.

s.to_room_name: A Shallow Section of the River

49122.name: A Shallow Section of the River
49122.description: The forest grows in close to the river, which begins to thin out here. The river is also becoming quite shallower, and in places you can see the bedrock poking up through the water, though this is closer to the banks. Sticking to about the center of the river, you are able to pick out a route deep enough to avoid damaging the bottom of your craft on the jagged rocks below.

49123.name: Along a Tranquil River
49123.description: Here the forest recedes quite far from the river. This is indeed a very tranquil spot, and the clearance of the trees affords a beautiful view of the wintery land and water about you. Small blocks of ice float gently along the river, and up near the banks the grass is frosted over in a fine dew, shimmering like thousands of wet crystals.

n.to_room_name: A Shallow Section of the River

s.to_room_name: The End of a Tranquil River

49124.name: The End of a Tranquil River
49124.description: The forest is now a goodly distance away from the water. East and west of the river, flat grasslands extend more than forty yards from the banks before the forest begins again. To the south the river opens up into a small lake, shimmering like glass in the distance.

49125.name: Atop a Glassy Lake
49125.description: Looking about your craft, you would swear you were atop a giant mirror if you didn't know better. A very thin layer of ice covers most of the lake, cracking as the water near it and under it is disturbed by your passing. The lake continues to stretch to the east, and the river feeding it flows in from the north.

n.to_room_name: The End of a Tranquil River

49126.name: Atop a Glassy Lake
49126.description: You gaze down curiously from the side of your boat and are met by a near perfect reflection of yourself. After a moment, you quickly tear your eyes away. Your reflection seems soulless... dead. Strangely, a small land mass rises out of the middle of the lake.

49128.name: On the Ledge
49128.description: You continue to walk along the ledge. In the distance to the north you see a large cave entrance as the ledge leads back into the mountain, to the south the ledge continues around toward the much safer path that leads back to the base of the mountain.

49129.name: On the Ledge
49129.description: You step very carefully as you walk out onto the ledge. You would not want to fall, it's a LONG way down. The cliff face curves around ahead of you, making the walk that much more difficult. The one thing that makes it bearable is the view: it is spectacular. You can see for miles. It makes you feel as if you're standing on top of the world.

49130.name: A Hidden Mountain Path
49130.description: The path winds its way up the mountain, leading out onto a ledge on a cliff face to the west. Rocks line the side of the path where you walk. The higher you climb on the mountainside, the more of the valley you can see. Deep blue mineral veins pulse faintly with color.

49131.name: Between the Boulders
49131.description: Walking between the two boulders is not as easy as one might think. They are close together, with barely enough room to walk between them, and snow has built up to a depth of several feet, forcing you to wade through it and making your journey that much harder.

49132.name: The Bottom of the Mountain
49132.description: The trees thin out this close to the mountainside to the west, which means that the snow gets deeper the further you go. The mountainside is covered in huge boulders, apparently blocking you from progressing any more. You can barely see a small chapel through the trees some distance to the east.

49133.name: Falling to your Death
49133.description: As your foot meets no resistance and you start to fall, you realize what a stupid idea it was to try to walk on air. OUCH!! That hurt! You have DIED! Tough break... A daemonic red robe has been damaged! A pair of daemonic red sandals has been damaged! Shuffled off the Mortal Coil You are dead. Far beneath you, you perceive the great body of Ishar and the powerful sun, Rymaras, slowly circling it. Ishar's tiny moons dance lightly around awaiting their next brief encounter with Saorin. You reflect on your affair with life and vow to pay more attention to your fragile existence. Wishing to return, you reach a hand out to enter Ishar. No exits are apparent. You fully rejoin the realm of the living. i You are carrying:

49134.name: A Small Island
49134.description: You stand on a small island in the lake. A thin layer of snow covers the ground here, and ice cracks under your feet. It is slightly warmer here due to the water which crashes gently against the shore, creating a soothing sound. The island is completely bare, save for a broken statue on the northeast tip of land.

49138.name: Outside a Stone Temple
49138.description: You stand outside a large circular building made of stone. No path leads here, and any that would have been beaten out by hundreds of feet making the journey has long since been blown over by the snow that drifts ceaselessly from the sky. The stones are all perfectly cut, and the building lacks any sense of the modesty and simplicity that dominates the rest of the structures in the forest.

49139.name: A Stone Entryway
49139.description: The walls inside are made from the same type of stone which composed the outside of the building, cut to the same incredible level of workmanship. The entryway is quite plain, and bears no adornments. The stone ceiling lies about ten feet from the ground, yet outside the building appeared much taller. Two hallways wrap along the circular walls heading clockwise and counterclockwise, and appear to be the only way further into the temple.

49140.name: The Hallway of Day
49140.description: The grey stone which you are familiar with has been replaced by an incredible pure white marble. The sun is inlaid with gold in the wall, and several tiny birds are inlaid in silver. A few torches mounted on the walls cast a small amount of light which is brilliantly magnified by the white stone, making this hallway quite bright despite it lacking windows or any source of natural light.

49141.name: The Hallway of Day
49141.description: The dazzling white marble hallway continues to wrap around here. The stonework and inlaid metalwork are masterpieces - quite a strange sight here in this ancient forest. It is much warmer here than outside, and you stop briefly to wipe the sweat from your face and loosen up your cloak. Up ahead, you can see the dull grey stone which made up the entryway and the outside of the building where the hall terminates.

49142.name: The Hallway of Night
49142.description: The grey stone which composed the outside of the building and the entryway has been replaced by a much darker material. You are unable to determine if it is black or a very deep blue, but it seems to suck up the small amount of light afforded by the still burning torches in the walls. The effect only accentuates the gold and silver inlaid into the walls and floor, a lavish depiction of the night sky.

49143.name: The Hallway of Night
49143.description: The exquisite stonework hallway continues to stretch along the building. You stop for a brief moment to enjoy the scene. However, your knowledge of astronomy is nothing to be impressed by, and you're unable to discern if this is an accurate depiction of the cosmos or not. Up ahead you can just make out the same grey stone that stood in the entryway.

49144.name: A Junction in the Hallways
49144.description: You stand in a small area of the grey stone that made up the entryway of the temple. It forms a modest square contrasting sharply to the elegance of the hallways of day and night. A simple high arched doorway leads into a large circular room to the north.

49145.name: Inside the Stone Temple
49145.description: One large circular room makes up the inside of the stone temple. The tiled stone floors are of magnificent beauty - they are unadorned, yet the stone is so perfectly cut and laid. Wooden tables and chairs are scattered about the room, and the walls are mostly lined by alternating lavish tapestries and bookcases full of holy writings and treatises on magic. Two stone stairways opposite each other spiral upwards and downwards, indicating multiple levels.

49146.name: Top of the Stone Temple
49146.description: Several circular windows of incredibly fine glass lie in a large stone dome the temple. The floor is a rich flawless white marble. The effect of the natural light spilling in through the many windows and reflecting off the many brilliant surfaces is dazzling. Several windows also stand in the walls, contrasting the first level of the temple, and the many portals to the outside world are just over the treetops, affording a magnificent view of the heavens. The priests and followers must've used this room for prayer and meditation. Fragments of a fallen star are embedded in the surrounding stone.

49147.name: A Subterranean Stone Hallway
49147.description: A long circular hallway of unadorned grey stone wraps almost all the way around the lower level of the temple. Several torches set into the wall light the way, yet it is still quite dark. Sporadically your vision blackens completely, and you stagger as you can see nothing beyond an inch of your nose, then suddenly everything goes back to normal. Must be your mind playing tricks on you.

49148.name: Under the Stone Temple
49148.description: You stand in a small antechamber of plain grey stone. A few wooden chairs line the walls, and to the south a great stone door stands in a wide doorway which has torches set at either side. Examining the doorway, you notice it seems to have no handles or keyholes, only a star-shaped indentation in the very center.

49149.name: Southwest Corner of the Chapel
49149.description: The corner of the chapel has been worn down by the elements over the year so that it is actually more curved than angled. The trees here have grown right up next to the building since there has been no one around to cut them back.

49150.name: In the White Forest
49150.description: The forest is filled with the sounds of wild animals. You hear birds singing in the trees and various animals moving through the brush around you. You are surrounded by tall, snow covered trees. A small stone building lies a short distance to the east.

49151.name: In the White Forest
49151.description: You must wind your way between the many types of trees as you walk through the forest. Snow trickles down on you as the branches move in the wind. Several small game trails lead off into brush too thick to follow them.

49078.name: The Top of the Stairs
49078.description: You stand at the top of the staircase. The stairs lead down to the main floor. Hallways lead off to the northeast and the southeast, leading to the rooms of the leaders and administrators of the order.

49079.name: The Northern Hallway
49079.description: The floor is covered with a fine red carpet. Your feet make no sound as you walk down the hallway. Tapestries adorn the wall to make up for the lack of doorways as there are no rooms in this stretch of hallway. The hallway continues to the north, or leads back to the stairway to the southwest.

49080.name: The Northern Hallway
49080.description: You stand at a bend in the hallway. Further down the hallway to the east you can see a set of double doors leading into someone's chambers. The carpet's condition gets worse the closer you get to the doors. You notice a deterioration in the condition of the tapestries on the wall as well.

49081.name: The Northern Hallway
49081.description: The hallway is in very bad shape. Water has been leaking though the roof for centuries, ruining the tapestries and rotting the carpet. The door appears to have been attacked with an axe or sword by someone trying to get through it. Boards have been nailed over the big holes to provide a modicum of privacy again.

49082.name: The High Priest's Chambers
49082.description: The first thing you see when you enter the room is the head of the former High Priest staring at you. It is nailed to the wall across from the doors and somehow, never decayed after being cut from his body. An arm and a leg are nailed to each of the side walls, and the body is nailed to the ceiling. Strangely, the pieces are all still bleeding. You only manage to not vomit by contemplating what you hope to accomplish in the waking hell of this accursed forest.

49083.name: The Southern Hallway
49083.description: The hall ends here. To the east, the hallway has collapsed, forever blocking off whatever might have been there before. You can see the bottom corner of a door just at the edge of the wreckage, but the pieces are too big to move out of the way to get through it.
